zh: 海内存知己，天涯若比邻。
en: A bosom friend afar brings a distant land near.

zh: 许多人会走进又走出你的生活，但只有真正的朋友会在你心中留下足迹。
en: Many people will walk in and out of your life, but only true friends will leave footprints in your heart.

zh: 你生命中的人就像你门廊上的柱子。
en: The people in your life are like the pillars on your porch.

zh: 有时他们支撑着你，有时他们依靠着你。
en: Sometimes they hold you up, and sometimes they lean on you.

zh: 有时知道他们就在身边就足够了。
en: Sometimes it's enough to know they are standing by.

zh: 朋友在困难时展现他们的爱，而不是在幸福时。
en: Friends show their love in times of trouble, not in happiness.

zh: 自从我离开学校后就再没见过詹金斯先生，但我经常想起他。
en: I haven't seen Mr Jenkins since I left school, but I often think about him.

zh: 在遇到詹金斯先生之前，我在大多数学校科目上都不太擅长。
en: I wasn't very good at most school subjects before I met Mr Jenkins.

zh: 我想我有点懒，尤其是在数学方面。
en: I suppose I was a bit lazy, especially in maths.

zh: 我在学校数学中唯一能记住的就是三角形的内角和是180度！
en: The only thing I can remember from school maths is that the angles of a triangle add up to 180 degrees!

zh: 但当我15岁进入詹金斯先生的班级时，我才第一次真正对一门学科产生了兴趣。
en: But when I was 15 and went into Mr Jenkins’ class, I really became interested in a subject for the first time.

zh: 在詹金斯先生教我之前，科学对我来说只是一门充满陌生词汇的学科。
en: Before Mr Jenkins taught me, science had simply been a subject full of strange words to me.

zh: 我不知道氢是什么，而且我也不想知道！
en: I had no idea what hydrogen was, and I didn't really want to know, either!

zh: 我觉得这一切既无聊又困难。
en: I found it all so boring and difficult.

zh: 但詹金斯先生让一切都变得有趣。
en: But Mr Jenkins made everything interesting.

zh: 他过去常常用大量的实际例子和简单的语言来解释那些看似困难的事情。
en: He used to explain things which seemed difficult with lots of practical examples and in simple language.

zh: 有一天，他带我们出去，我们造了一个火箭！
en: One day, he took us outside, and we built a rocket!

zh: 我记得他让我往火箭里倒了一些燃料，然后另一个学生点燃了一根火柴把它发射出去。
en: I remember that he let me pour some fuel into the rocket, and then another student lit a match to set it off.

zh: 我知道我不是一个自愿学习的学生，但我学习新东西并不慢。
en: I know that I wasn’t a willing student, but I wasn’t slow to learn new things.

zh: 问题在于我缺乏自信。
en: The problem was that I lacked confidence in myself.

zh: 詹金斯先生让我觉得自己有自己的优势。
en: Mr Jenkins made me feel that I had my own strengths.

zh: 我对研究恒星和行星很感兴趣，他让我给全班做一次演讲。
en: I was interested in the study of the stars and planets and he asked me to give a presentation to the class.

zh: 那真的是我第一次尝试向观众解释科学，而现在这就是我的工作！
en: That was really the first time I tried to explain science to an audience and now it's my job!

zh: 经常当我在准备一个节目时，我会想詹金斯先生会怎么做。
en: Often when I'm preparing a programme, I think about how Mr Jenkins would have done it.

zh: 有时我想，要是我能打电话给他征求他的意见就好了！
en: Sometimes I think, if only I could call him and ask for his opinion!

zh: 我读过几本格雷厄姆的书，也在电视上看到过他。
en: I've read a couple of Graham's books and seen him on TV.

zh: 我总是对我妻子说：“看，我以前教过他！”
en: I always say to my wife, “Oh look, I used to teach him!”

zh: 我记得格雷厄姆在进入我的班级之前非常难管教。
en: I remember Graham was very difficult before he came into my class.

zh: 我听说过他行为不端的故事。
en: I had heard stories about his bad behaviour.

zh: 有一次我抓到他和他的朋友们比赛谁能从学校舞台上跳得最远！
en: Once I caught him and his friends seeing who could jump the farthest off the school stage!

zh: 但当他产生兴趣后，他改变了。
en: But when he got interested, he changed.

zh: 他第一天走进我的教室时，把书包拖在身后，看起来很无聊，但当我用一个酸和一个洋葱做了一个展示人类胃如何工作的实验时，他立刻全神贯注地听我讲。
en: The first day he walked into my class, he was dragging his schoolbag behind him and looking bored, but as soon as I set up an experiment to show how the human stomach works using acid and an onion, he gave me his full attention.

zh: 他非常聪明，在科学科目上做得非常好。
en: He was very bright and he had done very well in science subjects.

zh: 然而，我并非对所有学生都像对格雷厄姆那样成功。
en: However, I have not done as well with all my students as I have with Graham.

zh: 我认为重要的是要明白，没有所谓的好学生或坏学生。
en: I think it's important to understand that there's no such thing as a good or a bad student.

zh: 每个人都擅长某事，重要的是找出每个学生擅长的是什么。
en: Everyone is good at something and it's important to find out what that is for each student.

zh: 我们教师应该有更多的时间与所有学生交朋友，真正理解他们。
en: We teachers should have more time to make friends with all our students and really understand them.

zh: 这样我们就能确保为他们所有人找到通往成功的道路，无论是在学校还是在以后的生活中。
en: Then we could make sure that we would find the path to success, both at school and in later life, for all of them.

zh: 我知道我选择了一份压力很大的工作，但我热爱我所做的事情。
en: I knew I had chosen a job with a lot of stress but I love what I do.

zh: 当老师的好处在于，你可以在孩子们思想开放、渴望学习的时候接触到他们的思想。
en: The thing about being a teacher is that you have access to children’s minds when they are open and eager to learn.

zh: 如果我作为老师所做的一切能帮助像格雷厄姆这样的孩子变成如此成功的成年人，那么我就知道我所做的是值得的。
en: If what I do as a teacher can help turn a child like Graham into such a successful adult, then I know what I’m doing is worthwhile.

zh: 正如著名教育家约翰·杜威所说：“教育不是为生活做准备；教育就是生活本身。”
en: As John Dewey, the famous educationalist, said, “Education is not preparation for life; education is life itself.”

zh: 在詹金斯先生教我之前，科学对我来说只是一门充满陌生词汇的学科。
en: Before Mr Jenkins taught me, science had simply been a subject full of strange words to me.

zh: 格雷厄姆在进入我的班级之前非常难相处。
en: Graham was very difficult before he came into my class.

zh: 我听说过关于他不良行为的故事。
en: I had heard stories about his bad behaviour.

zh: 我知道我选择了一份压力很大的工作。
en: I knew I had chosen a job with a lot of stress.

zh: 马克从未停止与他500个在线朋友社交。
en: Mark never stops socialising with his 500 friends online.

zh: 但他也从未感到如此孤独。
en: But he's also never felt more alone.

zh: 我在哪里？我在做什么？如果你是我500个在线朋友之一，你总是会第一个知道。
en: Where am I? What am I doing? If you're one of my 500 friends online, you'll always be the first to know.

zh: 我的手机和笔记本电脑从未离开过触手可及的距离，所以我不断地在社交媒体上发布更新——无论我是在喝咖啡、在上学路上、看电视……甚至在我洗澡的时候。
en: My phone and laptop are never out of touching distance, so I’m constantly posting updates on social media — whether I'm having a coffee, on my way to school, watching TV ... even when I'm in the shower.

zh: 早上我一睁开眼睛，就会查看我所有的社交网络应用，阅读我的电子邮件并回复短信。
en: As soon as I open my eyes in the morning, I check through all my social networking apps, read my emails and answer text messages.

zh: 我在吃早餐时又会重复做同样的事情。
en: I do the same thing all over again while I'm having breakfast.

zh: 我和几个很棒的室友住在一个大学宿舍里。
en: I live in a university dorm with a couple of great roommates.

zh: 我有来自所有我在线联系的人源源不断的信息和更新。
en: I have a never-ending flow of messages and updates from all the people I associate with online.

zh: 然而事实的真相是：我感到孤独。
en: Yet the truth of the matter is: I feel lonely.

zh: 我几乎不是唯一一个有这种感觉的人。
en: I'm barely the only person who feels this way.

zh: 超过三分之二的年轻人发现网上交友比'在现实生活中'更容易。
en: Over two-thirds of young people find it easier to make friends online than it is 'in real life'.

zh: 我远不止是一个害羞或内向的人，但我每天都像我的大多数朋友一样连接在网上。
en: I'm way beyond a shy or reserved person, but I'm wired up every day, like most of my friends.

zh: 表面上看，我有着活跃的社交生活。
en: On the surface, I have an active social life.

zh: 我参加聚会和做运动，但我总是心不在焉。
en: I attend parties and play sports but I’m always distracted.

zh: 他们说手机让人们更紧密地联系在一起，但实际上，我的思绪总是在千里之外。
en: They say that phones bring people closer together but in reality, my mind is always a million miles away.

zh: 我痴迷于我的账户上到底有多少关注者，但我却记不住一些最老朋友的生日。
en: I obsess over exactly how many followers I have on my account, but I can’t remember the birthdays of some of my oldest mates.

zh: 社交网络在很多方面主导着我的生活。
en: Social networking dominates my life in so many ways.

zh: 有时我给自己设定最后期限：我将在晚上8点开始做作业，并目标在两小时内完成。
en: Sometimes I set deadlines for myself: I will start doing my homework at 8 pm, and aim to finish in two hours.

zh: 晚上7点50分，我仍在浏览朋友们愚蠢的帖子和照片。
en: At 7.50 pm, I'm still scrolling through my friends’ silly posts and photos.

zh: 在我意识到之前，已经是晚上9点30分了，我仍然在沙发上一寸未动。
en: Before I know it, it’s 9.30 pm and I still haven't moved an inch from the sofa.

zh: 我下载了一首又一首歌，一个又一个视频，但我并没有真正注意它们。
en: I download song after song, video after video, but I'm not really paying attention.

zh: 刺激在于追逐下一首歌，而不是真正享受它。
en: The stimulation is in chasing after the next song rather than truly enjoying it.

zh: 我的朋友们试图把我从手机旁拉开，但一旦我又独自一人，我就拼命想回到网络世界和它提供的紧张活动中。
en: My friends try to drag me away from my phone, but as soon as I'm alone again, I'm desperate to get back to the online world and the intense activity that it provides.

zh: 我不断地感到沮丧、不满和孤独。
en: I constantly feel depressed, dissatisfied, and alone.

zh: 由于我花太多时间在网上社交，我不断拖延我现实生活中的重要事情：作业、任务、与朋友和家人进行有意义的联系。
en: Since I spend so much time socialising online, I keep delaying things that are important in my real life: homework, tasks, connecting with my friends and family members in a meaningful way.

zh: 有趣的是，我和我的朋友们在网上聊得那么多，但当我们见面时却最终无话可说。
en: It's funny that my friends and I chatter away online so much, but we end up having nothing to say when we meet.

zh: 几天前，我和一些朋友出去聚餐。
en: A few days ago, I went out for a dinner get-together with some friends.

zh: 我最好的朋友离开了餐桌30分钟，因为他必须接一个电话。
en: My best friend left the table for 30 minutes because he had to take a call.

zh: 有些人整个晚餐都弯着腰看手机，在网上给朋友发短信，却忽略了就坐在他们面前的人。
en: Some spent the dinner bending over their phones, texting friends online but ignoring the ones who sat right in front of them.

zh: 而不同寻常的是，没有人认为这是粗鲁的；这只是如今的生活方式。
en: And the extraordinary thing is nobody thought this was rude; it’s just how life is nowadays.

zh: 在去年夏天的一次家庭度假中，我妹妹把所有时间都花在抱怨她需要给手机充电上。
en: On a family holiday last summer, my sister spent all her time complaining that she needed to charge her phone.

zh: 当手机充好电后，她只是花了几个小时阅读关于她最喜欢的流行歌星的消息；她一次也没有抬起头来欣赏窗外的景色。
en: When it was charged, she just spent hours reading about her favourite popstar; not once did she lift her head to enjoy the view outside her window.

zh: 真正令人担忧的是，我认识的没有人，包括我自己，能够彻底戒掉。
en: What is really worrying is that no one I know, including myself, could go cold turkey.

zh: 我甚至无法想象一周不用社交网络——想想你会错过的所有重要约会、邀请和新闻更新！
en: I can’t even imagine going without social networking for a week — think of all the important appointments, invitations and news updates you would miss!

zh: 想戒酒的酗酒者可以避开酒，但我们如何放弃我们的手机？
en: Alcoholics who want to quit drinking can avoid booze, but how do we give up our phones?

zh: 毕竟，我学习需要它，因为我的老师和同学需要随时联系我。
en: After all, I need it for my studies because my teachers and classmates need to contact me at any time.

zh: 所以，这就是社交网络的问题。
en: So, that’s the problem with social networking.

zh: 我们被牢牢地连接着，但我们却比以往任何时候都更加 disconnected（疏离）。
en: We're hard-wired in, but we're more disconnected than ever.

zh: 我代表学校学生会写信。
en: I am writing on behalf of the school's Student Union.

zh: 您可能知道，我们正在举办全校范围的演讲比赛，决赛将于5月10日（上午9点至12点）在演讲厅举行。
en: As you may know, we are running a school-wide public speaking competition, and the final will take place at the lecture hall on 10 May (9 am-12 pm).

zh: 我写信是想询问您是否愿意帮助我们评判决赛。
en: I am writing to ask whether you would be willing to help us judge the final competition.

zh: 自三月以来，我们已经进行了两轮比赛，有超过100名学生参加。
en: Since March, we have already had two rounds of the competition, with more than 100 students.

zh: 现在我们有12名学生进入决赛。
en: Now we have 12 students left for the final.

zh: 决赛的演讲主题是“创新教育”。
en: The topic of the final speech is “Education for Innovation”.

zh: 学生们必须用英语就该主题进行五分钟的演讲。
en: Students have to give a five-minute speech on the topic in English.

zh: 在决赛期间，作为评委，您将负责聆听所有演讲，并记录下每个演讲的积极或消极方面。
en: During the final round, as a judge, you would be responsible for listening to all the speeches, noting down any positive or negative aspects for each one.

zh: 参赛者应根据他们的发音、清晰度和流利度，以及思想的推进和观众参与度来评判。
en: Competitors should be judged on their pronunciation, clarity and fluency, and also progression of ideas and audience engagement.

zh: 然后您将与其他三位评委讨论您的笔记，并决定谁获得第一、第二和第三名。
en: Then you will discuss your notes with three other judges, and come to a decision about who comes first, second and third.

zh: 您是否有时间在5月8日下午4点参加在学生会议室举行的简短会议，以便我们向您介绍其他评委并讨论细节？
en: Would you have time to attend a short meeting at the Student Union Room at 4 pm on 8 May so that we can introduce you to the other judges and discuss the details?

zh: 我们不会占用您太多时间。
en: We would not take up too much of your time.

zh: 我真诚地希望您能来。
en: I sincerely hope you can come.

zh: 如果您有任何问题，请务必告诉我。
en: If you have any questions, please do let me know.

zh: 什么是同伴压力，为什么会发生？
en: What is peer pressure and why does it happen?

zh: 我们都想成为群体的一部分，并感觉我们属于我们的社区。
en: We all want to be part of a group and feel like we belong to our community.

zh: 当我们因为想被同伴接受而受到影响去做一些我们通常不会做的事情时，同伴压力就可能发生。
en: Peer pressure can happen when we are influenced to do something that we would not usually do because we want to be accepted by our peers.

zh: 儿童和年轻人尤其感到要顺从同伴群体的社会压力。
en: Children and young adults especially feel social pressure to conform to the peer group.

zh: 同伴压力如何影响人们？
en: How does peer pressure affect people?

zh: 同伴压力可以影响人们的穿着、说话方式、听的音乐、采取的态度以及行为方式。
en: Peer pressure can influence how people dress, how they talk, what music they listen to, what attitudes they adopt and how they behave.

zh: 青少年希望被喜欢、融入并被接受，这意味着同伴压力可能很强大且难以抗拒。
en: Teenagers want to be liked, to fit in and to be accepted, which means peer pressure can be powerful and hard to resist.

zh: 缺乏自信和对自己不确定的人可能更倾向于通过附和风险建议或选择“错误”的道路来寻求同伴的认可。
en: People who are low in confidence and unsure of themselves may be more likely to seek their peers’ approval by going along with risky suggestions or choosing the “wrong” path.

zh: 在一项研究中，一个知道问题正确答案的学生实际上给出了错误的答案，因为班上其他所有人都给出了错误的答案，而他不想与众不同。
en: In one study, a student who knew the correct answer to a question actually gave the wrong answer because all the others in the class gave the wrong answer and he didn't want to be different.

zh: 同伴压力会导致欺凌吗？
en: Can peer pressure lead to bullying?

zh: 同伴压力肯定在欺凌中扮演了一个角色。
en: Peer pressure definitely plays a role in bullying.

zh: 如果一个青少年被大多数同伴视为软弱或与众不同，他们就可能成为欺凌者的目标。
en: If a teenager is seen as weak or different by the majority of their peers, they can become a target for bullies.

zh: 欺凌者挑选容易的目标——那些在同伴群体之外且群体不太可能为其辩护的人。
en: Bullies pick easy targets — people who are outside the peer group and whom the group is unlikely to defend.

zh: 不幸的是，一些欺凌者很受欢迎，甚至可能领导同伴群体，这意味着其他人不太可能挑战他们的行为。
en: Unfortunately, some bullies are popular and may even lead the peer group, which means others are less likely to challenge their behaviour.

zh: 学生可以做些什么来避免同伴压力？
en: What can students do to avoid peer pressure?

zh: 最重要的是建立自信，这样对同伴群体说“不”就更容易了。
en: The most important thing is to build up self-confidence, so that it is easier to say “no” to the peer group.

zh: 学生可以通过明智地选择朋友来做到这一点。
en: Students can do this by choosing their friends wisely.

zh: 对青少年来说，让自己身边围绕着积极的榜样——那些不取笑他们，而是接受他们并且自己也很自信的人——是个好主意。
en: It's a good idea for teens to surround themselves with positive role models — people who don't make fun of them, but accept them and are confident themselves.

zh: 年轻人思考他们行为的后果也很重要。
en: It's also important for young people to think about the consequences of their actions.

zh: 如果他们屈服于同伴压力（并且可能做了一些他们内心深处知道是错误的事情），结果会是什么？
en: If they give in to peer pressure (and perhaps do something that they know deep down is wrong), what could be the result?

zh: 如果他们停下来思考后果，他们可能会做出不同的决定。
en: If they stop and think about the consequences, they might make a different decision.

zh: 每个人都有不同的个性。
en: Everyone has a different personality.

zh: 它使人成为独立且独特的个体。
en: It is what makes people individual and unique.

zh: 然而，存在一些共同的人格特质，让我们可以讨论“人格类型”。
en: However, there are some shared personality qualities which let us talk about "personality types".

zh: 最基础的人格差异之一是外向型与内向型。
en: One of the most basic personality differences is between extroverts and introverts.

zh: 这些术语被用于许多关于人格类型的理论中。
en: These terms are used in many theories about personality types.

zh: 它们是由重要的瑞士精神病学家卡尔·荣格（1875-1961）推广开来的，尽管并非他所发明。
en: They were made popular by the important Swiss psychiatrist Carl Jung (1875-1961), although he did not invent them.

zh: 外倾与内倾是描述一个人对世界态度的方式：他们是趋向于世界还是远离世界？
en: Extroversion and introversion are ways of describing a person's attitude towards the world: Do they move towards it or away from it?

zh: 外向者是向外看的人。
en: Extroverts are people who look outwards.

zh: 他们友善、合群、健谈、热情且自信。
en: They are friendly, sociable, talkative, enthusiastic and self-confident.

zh: 他们对一系列经历感兴趣，并喜欢与他人共度时光。
en: They are interested in a range of experiences and enjoy spending time with other people.

zh: 他们倾向于先行动，后思考。
en: They tend to act first, then think later.

zh: 当外向者感觉不好、缺乏动力或没有精力时，他们会向自身之外寻找原因。
en: When extroverts feel bad, unmotivated or without energy, they look outside themselves.

zh: 这意味着他们可能去购物、打电话给朋友或举办聚会。
en: This means they might go shopping, call friends or have a party.

zh: 典型的外向型职业存在于政治、销售、教学和管理领域。
en: Typical extrovert jobs are in politics, sales, teaching and management.

zh: 另一方面，内向者则向内看。
en: Introverts, on the other hand, look inwards.

zh: 他们独处时感觉更舒适，并且享受想法与思考。
en: They feel more comfortable alone and enjoy ideas and thinking.

zh: 他们矜持、安静、深思熟虑，并且可能害羞。
en: They are reserved, quiet, thoughtful and may be shy.

zh: 他们喜欢在行动前思考和斟酌，并且通常在说话前进行分析。
en: They like to think and consider before taking action, and will often analyse before speaking.

zh: 与外向者相反，他们重视更少但更深刻的经历。
en: In contrast to extroverts, they value fewer, but deeper experiences.

zh: 当感觉不好或有压力时，内向者会向自己内心寻找解决方法。
en: When feeling bad or stressed, introverts look inside themselves for solutions.

zh: 内向的人通常从事艺术家、作家、工程师和发明家等工作。
en: Introverts often work as artists, writers, engineers and inventors.

zh: 虽然性格类型不同，但大多数人的性格中同时具有外向和内向的特征，不过通常他们更偏向于其中一种类型。
en: Although personality types are different, most people have both extrovert and introvert characteristics in their personality, but often they are more one type than the other.

zh: 关于人们为什么基本上是外向或内向的，已经进行了一些有趣的研究。
en: There has been some interesting research into why people are basically extroverts or introverts.

zh: 有些人说外向者和内向者是天生而非后天培养的。
en: Some people say that extroverts and introverts are born not made.

zh: 其他人认为环境在塑造一个人的性格方面更为重要。
en: Others believe that the environment is more important in shaping one’s personality.

zh: 在不同文化中，对内向者和外向者的态度各不相同。
en: Attitudes towards introverts and extroverts vary in different cultures.

zh: 例如，美国人重视外向的品质。
en: For example, Americans value extrovert qualities.

zh: 然而，中欧和东南亚等文化更看重内向的特征。
en: However, cultures such as those in central Europe and southeast Asia regard introvert characteristics more highly.

zh: 有趣的是，研究表明，居住在岛屿上的人往往更内向。
en: Interestingly, research shows that people who live on islands tend to be more introverted.

zh: 研究还发现，外向的人比内向的人幸福水平更高。
en: Studies have also found that extroverts have higher happiness levels than introverts.

zh: 今天我想谈谈我的科学老师张老师。
en: Today I would like to speak about my science teacher Ms Zhang.

zh: 我当她的学生已经两年了。
en: I have been her student for two years.

zh: 张老师的课总是那么引人入胜。
en: Ms Zhang's lessons are always so engaging.

zh: 她用很多实际的例子向我们展示事情是如何发生的，这使得课程非常有趣。
en: She uses lots of practical examples to show us how things happen, which makes the lessons extremely interesting.

zh: 她也非常有耐心。
en: She is also very patient.

zh: 我记得有一次我们正在进行一个实验，我不小心把一些酸掉在了地上。
en: I remember once we were conducting an experiment and I accidentally dropped some acid on the floor.

zh: 张老师没有生气，而是处理得非常好，她保持冷静，并帮我清理干净。
en: Instead of being angry Ms Zhang handled it so well by staying calm and helping me clean it up.

zh: 她一直在努力与我们分享她对科学的热爱，我希望她知道她所有的努力都是值得的。
en: She is constantly working hard to share her love of science with us and I hope she knows that all of her efforts are worthwhile.

zh: 在此，我代表我的同学们和我自己，想对张老师说：“教师节快乐！”
en: Here, on behalf of my classmates and myself, I would like to say to Ms Zhang, “Happy Teacher's Day!”

zh: 我想和你们分享一个学生的故事。
en: I'd like to share with you a story of a student.

zh: 这件事发生在一个星期五的清晨。
en: It happened early on a Friday morning.

zh: 蒂娜，一个十六岁的女孩，像往常一样来到学校。
en: Tina, a sixteen-year-old girl, came to school as usual.

zh: 但当她走进教室时，她发现她的老师躺在地板上，不省人事。
en: But when she entered the classroom, she found her teacher on the floor, unconscious.

zh: 蒂娜很快注意到她的老师戴着一个手环，上面说她有糖尿病。
en: Tina quickly noticed that her teacher was wearing a bracelet which said she had diabetes.

zh: 蒂娜打电话给紧急服务部门，告诉他们她刚刚看到的情况。
en: Tina phoned the emergency services and told them what she had just seen.

zh: 救护车服务的一位医生告诉蒂娜，他正在派一辆救护车，但同时她应该在老师的包里找一支带胰岛素的针。
en: A doctor from the Ambulance Service told Tina that he was sending an ambulance, but in the meantime she should look for a needle with insulin in the teacher's bag.

zh: 找到针后，蒂娜按照医生告诉她的做了——给老师的腿部注射。
en: Having found the needle, Tina did what the doctor had told her — to inject her teacher in the leg.

zh: 因为她在救护车到达之前做了她能做的一切，她的老师得救了。
en: And because she had done everything she could before the ambulance arrived, her teacher was saved.

zh: 她没有告诉任何人发生了什么。
en: She has not told anyone what happened.

zh: 多亏了蒂娜的快速反应和冷静，她的老师没事了，所以她才能站在这里和你们分享这个故事。
en: Thanks to Tina's quick response and calmness, her teacher was fine, so that she can stand here and share this story with you.

zh: 你说得对——我就是那个老师！
en: You're right — I am the teacher!

zh: 英语从来不是我喜欢的科目，直到我加入了吴老师的班级。
en: English was never my favourite subject until I joined Mr Wu's class.

zh: 我缺乏信心，在别人面前说英语让我感到压力很大。
en: I lacked confidence and speaking English in front of others made me feel stressed.

zh: 我去年英语考试不及格，感到非常羞愧。
en: I failed an English exam last year and felt so ashamed.

zh: 从那时起，我把英语课和一种可怕的感觉联系在一起。
en: From then on, I associated English classes with a terrible feeling.

zh: 在我遇到吴老师之后，一切都改变了。
en: Everything changed after I met Mr Wu.

zh: 他给了我一些关于如何提高口语的好建议，这样我就能说得更流利，并且逐渐开始更自信地说话。
en: He gave me some great advice on how to improve my speaking so that I could speak more fluently and gradually I started to speak with more confidence.

zh: 上个月，我被要求在课堂上做一次演讲，这是以前我会讨厌做的事情。
en: Last month, I was asked to make a presentation in class, something that I would have hated doing before.

zh: 我没有感到尴尬，反而感觉很好，并且说话清晰。
en: Instead of feeling embarrassed, I felt great and spoke with good clarity.

zh: 我想真诚地感谢吴老师一直相信我并帮助我发挥潜力。
en: I would like to thank Mr Wu sincerely for always believing in me and helping me reach my potential.

zh: 陈阳：网上的友谊可以和面对面的友谊一样真实和有意义。
en: Chen Yang: friendships online can be just as real and meaningful as in person.

zh: 我有一些朋友，我每天在网上和他们交往。
en: I have some friends who I socialise with online every day.

zh: 我很了解他们，他们也了解我！
en: I know them well and they know me!

zh: 李珍：我不确定我同意你可以在没有在现实生活中见过对方的情况下拥有真正的友谊。
en: Li Zhen: I'm not sure I agree with that you can have a real friendship without meeting the person in real life.

zh: 除非你与他们面对面交谈，否则人们不会展现真实的自己。
en: People aren't really themselves unless you talk with them face to face.

zh: 陈阳：在我看来，有时在社交媒体上，人们会对彼此非常粗鲁，甚至谎报自己的身份。
en: Chen Yang: In my opinion, sometimes on social media people are really rude to each other or even lie about who they are.

zh: 张磊：嗯，我同意陈阳的观点。
en: Zhang Lei: Well, I agree with Chen Yang.

zh: 如今，社交网络主导了我们的生活。
en: Social networking dominates our lives nowadays.

zh: 我们需要接受这一点。
en: We need to accept that.

zh: 我有一些好朋友住在其他国家。
en: I have some good friends who live in other countries.

zh: 多亏了互联网，我们可以互相闲聊。
en: Thanks to the Internet, we can chatter away with each other.

zh: 我把他们视为我真正的朋友。
en: I consider them my true friends.

zh: 工作吧，就像你不需要钱一样。
en: Work like you don't need the money.

zh: 去爱吧，就像你从未受过伤害一样。
en: Love like you've never been hurt.

zh: 千里之行，始于足下。
en: A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.

zh: 成功的真正秘诀是热情。
en: The real secret of success is enthusiasm.

zh: 当你志在顶峰时，到达顶峰就更容易了。
en: It's easier to get to the top when you're shooting for the top.

zh: 对很多人来说，成为百万富翁是成功的象征，他们把这作为自己的终极目标。
en: For a lot of people, becoming a millionaire is a symbol of success and they set this as their ultimate goal.

zh: 他们花一半的时间梦想着致富和实现他们期望的成功的方法，另一半的时间则思考着一旦他们变富后将要做的所有事情。
en: They spend half of their time dreaming up ways of getting rich and achieving the success they expect, and the rest of their time thinking about all the things they will do once they become rich.

zh: 但是，所有的百万富翁在实现目标后，是否都得到了他们所寻找的成功感？
en: But do all millionaires get the sense of success they were looking for after they achieve their goals?

zh: 有些人在成为百万富翁后仍然担心钱的问题——他们努力工作变得富有，但之后他们需要继续赚钱，这样才不会失去他们已经获得的成功感。
en: Some continue to worry about money when they become millionaires — they worked hard to become wealthy, but then they need to continue making money so they don't lose the sense of success they have achieved.

zh: 然而，也有人背弃了他们的百万财富，在生活中找到了其他形式的成功。
en: However, there are people who have turned their backs on their millions and found other forms of success in their lives.

zh: 杰森·哈利就是一个例子。
en: Jason Harley is one example.

zh: 十六年前，杰森是一名大学教授，拥有一栋巨大的六居室房子和两百万美元的财富。
en: Sixteen years ago, Jason was a college professor with a huge six-bedroom house and a fortune of two million dollars.

zh: 今天，他住在一个小宿舍房间里，只有二手家具。
en: Today he lives in a small dormitory room with only second-hand furniture.

zh: 这里当然没有任何迹象表明杰森是一个富有且成功的人！
en: There are certainly no signs that Jason is a rich and successful man!

zh: 外面有一个小花园，里面有几棵果树，杰森很喜欢照料它们。
en: There is a small garden outside with a few fruit trees, which Jason enjoys taking care of.

zh: 他还种了一些蔬菜和一些花。
en: He also grows some vegetables and a few flowers.

zh: 他的衣服和许多其他东西都是从慈善商店买的。
en: He gets his clothes and a lot of other things from charity shops.

zh: 但贾森很欣赏这个改变。
en: But Jason appreciates this change.

zh: 他很乐意放弃富人的生活方式。
en: He is pleased to give up the lifestyle of a rich man.

zh: 他厌倦了被人看作过着成功的生活，一个拥有一切而许多人一无所有的人。
en: He was tired of being regarded as living a successful life, as a person who had everything while many people had nothing.

zh: 他做出了捐出所有钱的选择。
en: He made the choice to give all his money away.

zh: 对此，他说，这给他带来了幸福和生活中的成功感。
en: And on this, he said, brought him happiness and a sense of success in life.

zh: “我不需要很多钱。我不想买更多的东西。我已经足够了，”贾森说。
en: “I don't need a lot of money. I don’t want to buy more things. I have enough,” said Jason.

zh: “我想要的是简单生活并帮助他人。
en: “What I want is to live simply and be helpful to others.

zh: 几年前，我是个百万富翁，但我知道每年有数百万人死于饥饿，还有一些地方没有学校和老师。
en: A few years ago, I was a millionaire, but I knew millions of people died of hunger every year, and there were places with no schools and no teachers.

zh: 所以很多人永远无法上学。
en: So many people could never go to school.

zh: 他们没有机会学习和在生活中取得进步。
en: They didn’t have the chance to learn and get on in life.

zh: 这让我非常担忧，作为一个富人却无所作为，我感到非常内疚。”
en: This worried me a lot and I felt very guilty being a rich man without doing anything.”

zh: 因此他把所有的钱都捐给了慈善机构。
en: Therefore he gave away all his money to charities.

zh: 每当他有超过两千美元时，他就会把小额钞票送给当地街上的无家可归者。
en: Whenever he had more than two thousand dollars, he would give away small bank notes to homeless people in the streets of local areas.

zh: “这是一种满足感和真正的成功感，是我以前从未感受过的，”贾森说。
en: “It was a kind of satisfaction and a real sense of success that I had never felt before,” said Jason.

zh: “当然，很多人认为‘成功’意味着银行里有数百万存款或有一份高薪工作。
en: “Sure, many people think that ‘success’ means having millions in the bank or a well-paid job.

zh: 但我认为我的成就感来自于帮助他人和找到生活的真正意义。”
en: But I think my sense of achievement comes from helping others and finding true meaning in life.”

zh: 大多数人相信，当他们生活舒适并赚到足够的钱时，他们就不会有烦恼。
en: Most people believe when they are living comfortably and earning enough money, they will have no worries.

zh: 然而，大多数人从不觉得他们赚够了。
en: However, most people never feel they have earned enough.

zh: 贾森·哈利决定退出这种生活方式，他发现只有一点钱让他感到自由。
en: Jason Harley decided to drop out of this kind of lifestyle, and he discovered that having only a little money made him free.

zh: 他是否怀念过去的一些东西？
en: Are there any things he misses from his past?

zh: “不，我现在快乐多了，因为我在按照自己的价值观生活。
en: “No, I’m much happier now, because I am living according to my values.

zh: 成功不是用你拥有多少钱来衡量的，而是用你如何理解生活的真正意义来衡量的。”
en: Success is not measured by how much money you have but by how you understand the true meaning of life.”

zh: 我不需要很多钱。
en: I don't need a lot of money.

zh: 我不想买更多的东西。
en: I don’t want to buy more things.

zh: 我想要的是简单生活并帮助他人。
en: What I want is to live simply and be helpful to others.

zh: 几年前，我是一个百万富翁，但我知道每年有数百万人死于饥饿，还有一些地方没有学校和老师。
en: A few years ago, I was a millionaire, but I knew millions of people died of hunger every year, and there were places with no schools and no teachers.

zh: 很多人永远无法上学。
en: So many people could never go to school.

zh: 他们没有机会学习和在生活中取得进步。
en: They didn't have the chance to learn and get on in life.

zh: 这让我非常担忧，作为一个富人却无所作为，我感到非常内疚。
en: This worried me a lot and I felt very guilty being a rich man without doing anything.

zh: 这是一种满足感和真正的成功感，是我以前从未感受过的。
en: It was a kind of satisfaction and a real sense of success that I had never felt before.

zh: 不，我现在快乐多了，因为我在按照自己的价值观生活。
en: No, I'm much happier now, because I am living according to my values.

zh: 他的研究不仅帮助中国找到了消除饥饿的方法，还成为了最大的在线销售公司。
en: His research has not only helped China find a way to remove hunger but also has become the biggest online sales company.

zh: 50多年来，他不仅帮助中国找到了消除饥饿的方法，还成为了最大的在线销售公司。
en: For more than 50 years, he has helped China find a way to remove hunger but also has become the biggest online sales company.

zh: ……每个人都认为他疯了。
en: ... everybody thought he was crazy.

zh: 他的努力工作使他比其他人大有优势。
en: His hard work gave him a big advantage over other people.

zh: 大约五年时间，当他还是个青少年时，他的努力工作使他比其他人大有优势。
en: For about five years, when he was a teenager, his hard work gave him a big advantage over other people.

zh: 五岁时，诺瓦克·德约科维奇决定他不想成为球员。
en: At the age of five, Novak Djokovic decided he didn't want to be players.

zh: 24岁的德约科维奇赢得了球员。
en: Djokovic, aged 24, won players.

zh: J·K·罗琳没有听那些负面的评论。
en: J. K. Rowling didn't listen to the negative comments.

zh: ……在过去的十年里，J·K·罗琳一直是《》的作者。
en: ... for the last ten years, J. K. Rowling has been the author of.

zh: 为什么奥运选手要将自己推向极限？
en: Why do Olympic athletes push themselves to the limits?

zh: 作为一名奥运选手，我错过了生日聚会，破坏了家庭假期，并跳过了夜出活动，但放弃这么多是有充分理由的。
en: As one of the Olympic athletes, I miss birthday parties, ruin family holidays and skip nights out, but there is a very good reason for giving up so much.

zh: “我们很担心你，”我坐在桌子对面的朋友说。
en: “We're worried about you,” said my friend from across the table.

zh: 他担心我不再是一个有趣的一起训练的人，并且我面临着只有一枚世界冠军奖牌而无人分享的危险。
en: He was worried that I had no longer become fun to train with and that I was in danger of being left with only a world championship medal and no one to share it with.

zh: 我本可以生气或为自己辩护。
en: I could have become angry or defensive.

zh: 难道他们不知道在体育领域达到顶峰有多难吗？
en: Don't they know how hard it is to be at the top in sport?

zh: 难道他们不知道要达到那里需要付出什么吗？
en: Don't they know what it takes to get there?

zh: 然而，在内心深处，我知道他所说的一些内容是对的。
en: However, deep down I knew elements of what he was saying were right.

zh: 我总是很累，每次锻炼的精神强度似乎对大多数人来说都难以承受。
en: I was always tired and every workout had a mental intensity that seemed too much for most to handle.

zh: 我正在将极限和极端推向超出大多数人认为健康的范围。
en: I was pushing the limits and extremes beyond what most thought were healthy.

zh: 这些是我作为一名职业运动员每天所面临的恐惧。
en: These are the fears I face as a professional athlete on a day-to-day basis.

zh: 我这样做是为了谁？
en: Who am I doing this for?

zh: 多少是太多？
en: How much is too much?

zh: 我为什么要这样做？
en: Why am I doing this?

zh: 我怎么能这么自私？
en: How can I be so selfish?

zh: 这些牺牲是为了什么？
en: What are the sacrifices for?

zh: 我想起我错过的无数生日聚会和夜出活动，因为在法国为了无休止地寻找训练游泳池而毁了一次家庭假期，差点错过我哥哥的婚礼，因为我当时正忙于在约翰内斯堡市中心租车并开车穿过“禁区”，以便与当地铁人三项运动员进行团体骑行。
en: I think of countless birthday parties and nights out I have missed, ruining a family holiday in France because of the endless search for a swimming pool to train in, almost missing my brother's wedding because I was too busy hiring a car in downtown Johannesburg and driving through the “no-go” areas in order to do a group ride with the local triathletes.

zh: 归结起来就是：我天生拥有巨大的动力和决心。
en: It boils down to this: I was born with an enormous amount of drive and determination.

zh: 从小女孩时起，我就会在午餐时间待在室内，只是为了提前完成课堂作业，而不是出去玩；我十岁时就自己乘坐公交车，
en: From a small girl, I would stay in at lunch time just to get ahead on class work rather than go out and play; I would get the bus on my own at ten years old and

zh: 成为一名职业运动员也没什么不同。
en: Being a professional athlete is no different.

zh: 如果你想成为奥运冠军，关键在于你在准备过程中所做的那些额外的小事，这些小事将使你与竞争对手区分开来。
en: If you want to be an Olympic champion, it’s all about that little extra thing you have done in your preparation that will set you apart from your competitors.

zh: 这会被过度吗？绝对会。
en: Can this be overdone? Absolutely.

zh: 但如果你的梦想是成为最好的并登上冠军领奖台，你最好全身心投入到你的运动中。
en: But if your dream is to be the best and reach the winner's platform, you had better be totally committed to your sport.

zh: 用英国铁人三项运动员阿利斯泰尔·布朗利的话来说，即使这意味着受伤，“我宁愿有三四年出色的获胜经历，也不愿有十年平庸的表现。”
en: In the words of the British triathlete Alistair Brownlee, even if it means getting injured, “I'd prefer to have three or four outstanding years of winning stuff than having ten years of being average.”

zh: 作为运动员，我们所做的事情让我们与众不同，因为我们愿意多做那么一点点，这可能会让我们登上顶峰。
en: Doing what we do as athletes sets us apart because we are willing to do that extra little bit that might take us to the top.

zh: 这当然不是一种平衡的生活方式，也当然不正常，但这些词通常不会用在任何追求伟大的人身上。
en: It is certainly not a balanced way to live and it is certainly not normal but those words are generally not used for anyone who is striving for greatness.

zh: 为什么奥运选手要将自己推向极限？
en: Why do Olympic athletes push themselves to the limits?

zh: 我错过了生日派对，破坏了家庭假期，还跳过了晚上的外出活动。
en: I miss birthday parties, ruin family holidays and skip nights out.

zh: “我们很担心你，”我朋友从桌子对面说道。
en: "We're worried about you," said my friend from across the table.

zh: 难道他们不知道在体育领域达到顶峰有多难吗？
en: Don't they know how hard it is to be at the top in sport?

zh: ……因为我当时正忙于在约翰内斯堡市中心租车。
en: ... because I was too busy hiring a car in downtown Johannesburg.

zh: 我天生就拥有巨大的动力和决心。
en: I was born with an enormous amount of drive and determination.

zh: 我是一个拥有250名男孩的橄榄球俱乐部里唯一的女孩。
en: I was the only girl in a rugby club of 250 boys.

zh: 但如果你的梦想是成为最好的并登上冠军领奖台，你最好全身心投入到你的运动中。
en: But if your dream is to be the best and reach the winner's platform, you had better be totally committed to your sport.

zh: 郎平是中国最知名的运动员之一。
en: Lang Ping is one of China’s most recognised athletes.

zh: 她作为排球明星已超过三十五年，并为这项运动做出了巨大贡献，这使她成为中国排球史上最受欢迎的人物。
en: She has been a volleyball star for more than thirty-five years and has contributed greatly to the sport, which has made her the most popular figure in China’s volleyball history.

zh: 郎平在1980年代曾效力于中国国家排球队。
en: Lang Ping played on China’s national volleyball team during the 1980s.

zh: 她强劲有力的打球风格为她赢得了“铁榔头”的称号。
en: Her strong and powerful playing style earned her the name “Iron Hammer”.

zh: 郎平是这支球队的一员，当时她们取得了一系列成功，包括1984年奥运会金牌、1982年世界锦标赛金牌以及1981年和1985年世界杯金牌。
en: Lang was a member of the team when they achieved a series of success including gold medals at the 1984 Olympics, the 1982 World Championship and the 1981 and 1985 World Cups.

zh: 郎平因其成功、积极的态度和接受挑战的能力而成为中国的明星。
en: Lang Ping became a star in China because of her success, her positive attitude and her ability to take on challenges.

zh: 1986年作为运动员退役后，她作为排球教练在美国和意大利取得了成功。
en: After retiring as an athlete in 1986 she has been successful as a volleyball coach in the United States and Italy.

zh: 然而，郎平对中国排球的承诺使她回到了家乡。
en: However, Lang Ping’s commitment to Chinese volleyball brought her back home.

zh: 1995年，在球队陷入困境时，她回来担任了国家女队的教练。
en: In 1995, she returned to become the coach of the women’s national team when the team was struggling.

zh: 在她的领导下，球队重回巅峰，赢得了2015年世界杯。
en: Under her leadership, the team came back to the top, winning the 2015 World Cup.

zh: 那一年，郎平在央视体坛风云人物颁奖典礼上获得了最佳教练和体育特别贡献奖。
en: That year, Lang Ping was given the Best Coach and the Sports Special Contribution awards at the CCTV Sports Personality Awards.

zh: 她还被评为2016年“感动中国年度人物”。
en: She was also named “Touching China Personality” of 2016.

zh: 她之所以受人爱戴，不仅是因为她对事业的奉献，也是因为她对排球运动的巨大影响。
en: She is loved not only because of her devotion to her career, but also because of her huge influence on the sport of volleyball.

zh: 以其典型的坦率风格，理查德·圣约翰提醒我们，成功不是一条单行道，而是一段持续的旅程。
en: In his typically candid style, Richard St. John reminds us that success is not a one-way street, but a constant journey.

zh: 他用自己企业兴衰的故事来说明一个宝贵的教训。
en: He uses the story of his business’ rise and fall to illustrate a valuable lesson.

zh: 黄大年，中国著名地球物理学家，1958年出生于中国广西。
en: Huang Danian, the renowned Chinese geophysicist, was born in 1958 in Guangxi, China.

zh: 作为一名热情能干的学生，黄大年于1993年前往英国深造。
en: As a keen and able student, Huang went to the UK in 1993 to further his studies.

zh: 到2008年黄大年搬回中国时，他已经在英国生活和工作了15年。
en: By the time Huang moved back to China in 2008, he had been living and working in the UK for 15 years.

zh: 他在那里有一份好工作和生活，但他放弃了一切回到祖国，因为他有一个信念，他需要为他的国家做贡献。
en: He had a good job and a life there, but he gave it all up to return to home, driven by the idea that he needed to contribute to his country.

zh: 作为世界领先的深部地球探测技术专家之一，黄大年被邀请参加“千人计划”。
en: As one of the world’s leading experts in deep earth exploration technology, Huang was approached to participate in the “Thousand Talent” programme.

zh: 他在长春的吉林大学担任了一个职位。
en: He took up a position at Jilin University, Changchun.

zh: 黄大年被任命为中国深部地球探测项目的首席科学家，开发能够看穿地壳的先进相机，从而无需挖掘即可对其进行分析。
en: Huang was named lead scientist on China’s deep earth exploration programme, developing advanced cameras that can see through the Earth's crust so that it can be analysed without having to dig into it.

zh: 他建立了一个最先进的实验室，有时用自己的钱支付设备费用。
en: He set up a state-of-the-art lab, sometimes paying for equipment with his own money.

zh: 有些人形容他为“疯子”，但这种激情和动力使黄大年将中国的深部地球探测技术推向了世界领先地位。
en: Some described him as a “lunatic” (a madman), but this passion and drive enabled Huang to propel China’s deep earth exploration technology into a world-leading position.

zh: 黄大年的奉献精神促成了2013年中国月球探测器“玉兔”登陆月球以及2016年神舟十一号和天宫二号飞船的发射。
en: Huang’s dedication contributed to China’s lunar probe Yutu being landed on the moon in 2013 and the launch of the spacecrafts Shenzhou-11 and Tiangong-2 in 2016.

zh: 黄大年的健康也为他致力于工作付出了代价。
en: Huang's health also paid the price for his commitment to his work.

zh: 他在2012年开始出现昏厥，但对此很少关注，声称他没有时间去看医生——他的工作永远是第一位的。
en: He began having fainting fits in 2012, but paid them little attention, stating he did not have time to go to see a doctor — his work always came first.

zh: 2016年11月，黄大年昏倒并被送往医院，在那里他被诊断出患有癌症。
en: In November 2016, Huang collapsed and was taken to hospital, where he was diagnosed with cancer.

zh: 病情已非常严重，他只剩下几个月的生命。
en: The disease was so advanced that he had just a couple of months to live.

zh: 正如他是一位真正的科学家一样，黄大年从未放弃，而是始终努力向前推进。
en: Like the true scientist he was, Huang never gave up, but always tried to push forwards.

zh: 即使躺在医院的病床上，他仍然继续工作，为同事写推荐信，并回复学生的问题。
en: Even from his hospital bed, he continued his work, writing letters of reference for his colleagues and replying to questions from his students.

zh: 他对后起之秀的才能充满信心。
en: He had great faith in the talent of the up-and-coming generation.

zh: “我们国家急需人才，”他说。
en: “Our country is in urgent need of talented people,” he said.

zh: “如果我们花更多时间，更多地关注年轻人，大师甚至诺贝尔奖获得者可能会在他们当中涌现。”
en: “If we spend more time and pay more attention to the young, masters and even Nobel prize winners may rise among them.”

zh: 黄大年于2017年1月去世，年仅58岁。
en: Huang died in January 2017, aged just 58.

zh: 800多人参加了他的葬礼，以纪念一个燃烧得如此明亮却又如此短暂的生命。
en: More than 800 people attended his funeral to celebrate a life that burned so bright, but was so short.

zh: 当世界著名作家J·K·罗琳在哈佛大学毕业典礼上发表演讲时，一些人可能对她选择的主题感到有些惊讶：“失败的好处”。
en: When the world-famous author J. K. Rowling gave a speech at a Harvard University graduation ceremony, some may have been a little surprised by the topic she chose: “The Benefits of Failure”.

zh: 罗琳的第一部小说在被最终出版前遭到了12家出版商的拒绝，但矛盾的是，正是这段经历帮助她获得了成功：“失败教会了我关于自己的一些东西，这是我无法通过其他方式学到的。
en: Rowling's first novel was rejected by 12 publishers before it was finally published, yet paradoxically, it was this experience that helped her to succeed: “Failure taught me things about myself that I could have learnt no other way.

zh: 我发现我拥有坚强的意志，以及比我预期更多的自律。”
en: I discovered that I had a strong will, and more discipline than I had expected.”

zh: 已故的苹果公司首席执行官史蒂夫·乔布斯在其职业生涯早期经历了失败，当时他从自己共同创立的公司被解雇了。
en: The late Apple CEO Steve Jobs experienced failure early in his career, when he was fired from the company he had co-founded.

zh: 他感到了绝望和拒绝。
en: He felt despair and rejection.

zh: 然而，在他看来，这是“可能发生在我身上的最好的事情。
en: However, it was, in his view, “the best thing that could have ever happened to me.

zh: 成功的沉重感被再次成为初学者的轻松感所取代，对一切都不那么确定了。
en: The heaviness of being successful was replaced by the lightness of being a beginner again, less sure about everything.

zh: 它让我得以进入我生命中最富创造力的时期之一。”
en: It freed me to enter one of the most creative periods in my life.”

zh: 甚至比尔·盖茨也经历过失败。
en: Even Bill Gates has experienced failure.

zh: 他的第一家公司叫做Traf-O-Data，但不幸的是，它的产品无法工作。
en: His first company was Called Traf-O-Data, but unfortunately, its product didn’t work.

zh: 这是一场彻底的灾难。
en: It was a total disaster.

zh: 但盖茨从这次经历中学到的东西，为如今微软的全球成功奠定了基础。
en: But what Gates learnt from this experience helped lay the groundwork for the global success that is now Microsoft.

zh: 他没有两次犯同样的错误。
en: He didn’t make the same mistake twice.

zh: 才华横溢的时装设计师王薇薇建立了一个全球时尚帝国，然而生活并非总是如此一帆风顺。
en: Talented fashion designer Vera Wang has built a global fashion empire, yet life wasn't always so straightforward.

zh: 作为一名年轻时专注的花样滑冰运动员，王薇薇未能入选1968年美国奥运代表队，这让她感到极度失望。
en: A dedicated ice skater as a young woman, Wang was bitterly disappointed when she did not make the 1968 US Olympic team.

zh: 她放弃了体育事业，加入《Vogue》杂志担任助理编辑，但遭到了打击。
en: She gave up sport and joined Vogue as an assistant editor, but was crushed.

zh: 在杂志社工作了15年后，她错过了晋升为主编的机会。
en: After 15 years at the magazine, she was passed over for promotion to editor-in-chief.

zh: 然而，这次挫折促使她创立了自己的新娘时装品牌。
en: However, this setback prompted her to set up her own bridal fashion label.

zh: 李安，一位世界著名的导演，无论是作为学生还是演员，都未曾成功。
en: Ang Lee, a worldwide-famous director, wasn’t successful either as a student or as an actor.

zh: 在国内两次高考失利后，李安转而赴美学习戏剧，但他糟糕的英语阻碍了他成为一名演员。
en: Twice failing the college entrance examination at home, Lee turned to study drama in the US, but his poor English prevented him from becoming an actor.

zh: 在被导演专业录取后，李安花了六年时间寻找执导电影的机会，但只被要求照看拍摄设备。
en: After being accepted for a directing major, Lee spent six years looking for opportunities to direct a film, but was only asked to look after filming equipment.

zh: 随后，他大部分时间都在写剧本，但这些剧本在两周内被超过30家公司拒绝。
en: He then spent most of his time writing plays which were rejected by more than 30 companies within two weeks.

zh: 在六年没有收入的日子里，他不得不依靠他的妻子。
en: During six years with no income, he had to depend on his wife.

zh: 最终，在他妻子的鼓励和他自己的决心下，他所有的努力终于得到了回报。
en: In the end, with his wife’s encouragement and his determination, all his efforts finally paid off.

zh: 所有这些人都有一个共同点：在艰难时刻
en: All these individuals share something in common: At tough moments

zh: 简单来说，它是对某件事的热情以及努力工作并坚持下去的决心。
en: Put simply, it’s a passion for something and the determination to work hard and to stick with it.

zh: 当我们遇到挫折时很容易放弃，但有毅力的人会继续前进。
en: It's very easy to give up when we hit setbacks, but people who have grit keep going.

zh: 这就是失败的重要性所在：如果我们能学会如何应对失败，我们就能学会如何成功。
en: This is where the importance of failure comes in: if we can learn how to manage failure, we'll learn how to succeed.

zh: 通过经历一系列失败，我们能更好地为成年后将面临的更大挑战做好准备。
en: By working through a series of failures, we're better prepared for the bigger challenges we'll face in the adult world.

zh: 我们将拥有继续前进所需的适应力。
en: We'll have the resilience we need to keep going.

zh: 拥有很多钱就意味着成功吗？
en: Does having a lot of money mean being successful?

zh: 我认为杰森·哈利，一位教授和前百万富翁，可以被称为一个成功的人。
en: I think Jason Harley, a professor and an ex-millionaire, can be called a successful person.

zh: 许多人认为他一定是疯了，把钱送给无家可归的人，并且住在一个小宿舍里，用着二手家具。
en: Many people think he must be mad by giving away money to homeless people, and living in a small dormitory with second-hand furniture.

zh: 但对他来说，在他决定放弃他的财富之后，没有人比他过得更好。
en: But to him, no one has lived a better life than he has after he decided to turn his back on his fortune.

zh: 他说，尽管他拥有所有财富，但当他看到有些人挣得那么少，几乎无法养活自己时，他感到内疚。
en: He said that with all his fortune, he felt guilty when he saw there were people who earn so little and could barely support themselves.

zh: 在我看来，他是少数真正理解生活中真正成功是什么的人之一。
en: In my mind, he is one of the few people who really understand what real success is in life.

zh: 我真的很欣赏他的勇气和意愿做出如此巨大的改变。
en: I really appreciate his courage and willingness to make such a huge change.

zh: 得知杰森用最少的钱和东西生活却比以往任何时候都快乐，这很令人鼓舞！
en: It's encouraging to know that Jason is happier than ever living with a minimum amount of money and things!

zh: 当谈到关于成功的故事时，我个人认为谈论有影响力的人以及他们如何克服生活中的巨大困难是非常激励人心的。
en: When it comes to stories about success, I personally think it's very motivating to talk about influential people and how they overcame great difficulties in their lives.

zh: J.K.罗琳就是一个很好的例子——她的第一本书被拒绝了12次，最终才取得了巨大的成功。
en: J. K. Rowling is a good example — her first book was rejected 12 times before it eventually became a great success.

zh: 世界上一些最大公司的创始人，如马云和比尔·盖茨，在开始时也经历了挫折，但他们有实现目标的承诺。
en: Founders of some of the biggest companies in the world, like Ma Yun and Bill Gates, also experienced setbacks at the beginning, but they had the commitment to achieve their goals.

zh: 我认为如果我们努力建立自己的信心并努力工作，我们都能实现我们的目标。
en: I think if we try to build our own confidence and work hard, we can all achieve our goals.

zh: 当我完成学业时，我想成为一名职业运动员。
en: When I finish my studies, I would love to be a professional athlete.

zh: 从我小时候起，我就对篮球着迷，打篮球、看篮球、谈论篮球，一切！
en: Ever since I was a child, I have been obsessive about basketball, playing it, watching it, talking about it, everything!

zh: 有这么多其他有才华的年轻篮球运动员，所以我必须比他们多做一点额外的事情才能脱颖而出。
en: There are so many other talented young basketball players so I have to do that extra little bit more than them to stand out.

zh: 我也知道我将不得不做出许多牺牲，包括把我所有的空闲时间都用来练习，而不是去参加聚会。
en: I know too that I will have to make many sacrifices, including giving all my free time to practise instead of going to parties.

zh: 幸运的是，这很适合我的个性，因为我总是过着极端的生活，并把自己推到极限。
en: Luckily, it suits my personality as I have always lived my life to extremes and pushed myself to the limits.

zh: 我认为，为了在任何领域取得成功，你必须有勇气和决心去追求卓越。
en: I think that you have to have the courage and determination to strive for greatness in order to be successful in any field.

zh: 赚钱不是我生活中最重要的考虑因素。
en: Earning money is not the most important consideration in my life.

zh: 我认为有太多的人花时间担心如何达到顶峰，即使他们并不快乐。
en: I think too many people spend time worrying about trying to reach the top, even if they are not happy.

zh: 我将来想做的是护理工作。
en: What I would like to do in the future is nursing.

zh: 我知道在很多人眼里这并不成功。
en: I know it is not success in many people's eyes.

zh: 但对我来说，帮助人们康复就是成功。
en: But for me, helping people to recover is success.

zh: 我母亲总是告诉我，实现我的目标不如快乐重要。
en: My mother always told me that achieving my goals was not as important as being happy.

zh: 我完全同意她的观点。
en: I totally agree with her.

zh: 作为一名教师，我认为学生找到课外活动和学习之间的平衡很重要。
en: As a teacher, I think it is important for students to find a balance between extracurricular activities and their studies.

zh: 这样他们就会既快乐又成功。
en: Then they will be both happy and successful.

zh: 如果你想成为一名世界级的游泳运动员，你必须花很多时间在游泳池里。
en: If you want to become a world-class swimmer you have to spend a lot of time in swimming pools.

zh: 否则你永远不会成功。
en: You will never succeed otherwise.

zh: 商界领袖明白，没有优秀团队的支持，你就不可能成功。
en: Business leaders understand that you cannot become successful without the support of a good team.

zh: 我的员工使我成为一个伟大的老板。
en: My employees make me a great boss.

zh: 我曾是一名成功的记者，现在退休了。
en: I was a successful journalist and now I'm retired.

zh: 如果我能重活一次，我会花更多的时间和家人朋友在一起。
en: If I could relive my life, I would spend more time with family and friends.

zh: 我错过了很多重要的事件。
en: I missed so many important events.

zh: 在我上一份工作中，我花了太多时间在会议上，以至于我们很少有时间工作！
en: In my last job, I spent so much time in meetings that we seldom had time to work!

zh: 现在我有了自己的生意，我限制了会议的数量。
en: Now I have my own business and I limit the number of meetings.

zh: 保护是人类与土地之间的一种和谐状态。
en: Conservation is a state of harmony between men and land.

zh: 取之有度，用之有节，则常足；
en: Take with restraint and use in moderation; this way one shall always know contentment:

zh: 保护意味着为了人类的长远利益而明智地利用地球及其资源。
en: Conservation means the wise use of the Earth and its resources for the lasting good of men.

zh: 环境污染是一种不治之症。
en: Environmental pollution is an incurable disease.

zh: 它只能被预防。
en: It can only be prevented.

zh: 灭绝，即整个物种被彻底抹去，在我们地球的历史中并不罕见。
en: Extinctions, where entire species are wiped out, are not unusual in our Earth’s history.

zh: 事实上，所有曾经存在过的物种中，超过90%现在已经灭绝。
en: In fact, over 90 percent of all species that ever lived are now extinct.

zh: 在过去的5亿年里，地球上的生命几乎终结的情况已经发生了五次。
en: In the last 500 million years, there have been five times when life on Earth has almost ended.

zh: 这其中包括第三次大灭绝，即所谓的“大灭绝”，它导致了90%到96%的物种死亡。
en: These include the third mass extinction, known as the “Great Dying”, which killed 90% to 96% of all species.

zh: 而第五次事件发生在6600万年前，导致了恐龙的灭亡。
en: And the fifth event, which occurred 66 million years ago, caused the death of the dinosaurs.

zh: 灭绝的原因是多种多样的。
en: The reasons for extinctions are varied.

zh: 根据科学家的说法，它们包括火山喷发、小行星从太空撞击地球、海平面变化、海水含氧量下降以及全球变暖。
en: According to scientists, they include volcanic eruptions, asteroids hitting the Earth from space, changes in sea levels, the decrease of the oxygen content of the sea and global warming.

zh: 许多科学家表示，我们现在正进入地球的第六次大灭绝。
en: Many scientists say we are now entering the Earth's sixth mass extinction.

zh: 这一次，人类活动将受到指责。
en: This time, human activity will be to blame.

zh: 虽然物种自然消亡并不罕见，但如今这种情况发生的速度令人担忧。
en: Although it is not unusual for species to die out naturally, the rate at which this is now happening is cause for concern.

zh: 2015年，美国布朗大学和杜克大学的科学家进行了一项研究，考察了物种因自然原因灭绝的速度。
en: A 2015 study by scientists who were based at Brown University and Duke University in the US, looked at how fast species die out due to natural causes.

zh: 他们发现人类活动导致物种灭绝的速度比正常情况快1000倍。
en: They found that human activity is causing species to die out 1,000 times faster than normal.

zh: 许多物种灭绝的原因有很多：空气和水污染、森林被破坏、工厂化养殖和过度捕捞。
en: There is a long list of reasons why so many species are dying out: air and water pollution, forests being destroyed, factory farming and overfishing.

zh: 很明显，人类活动对地球上的所有其他物种，包括动物和植物，都产生了负面影响。
en: It is clear that human activity has negatively affected all other species on Earth, including animals and plants.

zh: 如果第六次大灭绝发生，研究这个问题的科学家认为，地球上多达四分之三的物种可能会灭绝。
en: If a sixth mass extinction occurs, scientists who have studied the issue believe that up to three quarters of all species on Earth could die out.

zh: 当然，由于我们人类的生存依赖于许多物种，我们也将面临灭绝的风险。
en: Of course, as we humans depend on so many species for our survival, we would also be at risk of dying out.

zh: 我们的世界和我们的生活依赖于自然界中动植物之间的平衡。
en: Our world and our lives depend on the balance in nature between animals and plants.

zh: 没有森林，我们将没有空气呼吸；没有干净的水，我们将无法生存。
en: Without forests, we would have no air to breathe; and without clean water we would be unable to survive.

zh: 黑知更鸟是人类从灭绝中拯救出来的鸟类。
en: Black robins are the birds that humans saved from extinction.

zh: 1980年，世界上只剩下五只黑知更鸟。
en: In 1980, there were only five black robins left in the world.

zh: 它们都生活在新西兰海岸附近的一个小岛上。
en: They all lived on a small island off the coast of New Zealand.

zh: 其中只有一只名叫老蓝的雌鸟能够产卵。
en: Only one of those birds, a female called Old Blue, was able to lay eggs.

zh: 野生动物工作者启动了一个饲养黑知更鸟幼鸟的计划。
en: Wildlife workers began a programme for raising black robin chicks.

zh: 他们保护老蓝的蛋，帮助它们孵化，并照顾幼鸟。
en: They protected Old Blue's eggs, helped them to hatch, and took care of the chicks.

zh: 由于他们的努力，现在有超过250只黑知更鸟，并且种群正在扩散。
en: Thanks to their efforts, there are now more than 250 black robins, and the population is spreading.

zh: 为了帮助它们生存，人们正在恢复它们的栖息地，并清除捕食它们的小动物，比如老鼠和猫，从它们居住的岛屿上。
en: To help them survive, people are restoring their habitats and removing small animals that hunt them, such as mice and cats from the islands where they live.

zh: 第六次大灭绝可以避免吗？
en: Can a sixth extinction be avoided?

zh: 专家表示，如果我们现在采取行动，还为时不晚。
en: Experts say that it is not yet too late, if we take action now.

zh: 这将意味着我们必须显著改变我们的生活方式。
en: This would mean we have to significantly change the way we live.

zh: 例如，我们需要采取措施拯救濒危物种，包括建立可以保护动植物的特殊区域。
en: For example, we need to take steps to save endangered species, including setting up special areas where plants and animals can be protected.

zh: 我们需要通过使用更清洁、更自然的能源，如风能和太阳能，来改变我们的工作和旅行方式。
en: We need to change how we work and travel by using cleaner, more natural sources of energy, such as wind and solar energy.

zh: 我们还需要改进我们的耕作方法，以减少水、空气和土地污染。
en: We also need to improve our farming methods to reduce water, air and land pollution.

zh: 简而言之，如果我们想生存，就需要停止破坏我们居住的星球，并开始保护它。
en: In short, if we want to survive, we need to stop destroying the planet that we live on and start to protect it.

zh: 在过去的5亿年中，地球上发生了五次大规模灭绝事件。
en: In the last 500 million years, there have been five mass extinction events on Earth.

zh: 在第三次事件中，被称为'大灭绝'，90%到96%的物种都灭绝了。
en: In the third event, which was known as the 'Great Dying', 90% to 96% of all species died out.

zh: 当然，物种灭绝是由于多种原因造成的。
en: Of course, species died out due to many causes.

zh: 但如今，人类活动已经影响到我们赖以生存的所有物种。
en: But nowadays, human activity has affected all types of species that we depend on for living.

zh: 我们需要采取行动以避免第六次大规模灭绝。
en: We need to take action to avoid the sixth mass extinction.

zh: 我们应该采取措施拯救濒危的动植物，并建立特殊区域来照顾它们，以免下一次可能消灭整个物种的灭绝事件到来。
en: We should take steps to save endangered animals and plants, and set up special areas to take care of them before the next extinction comes that may wipe out the entire species.

zh: 灭绝，即整个物种被消灭，在我们地球的历史中并不罕见。
en: Extinctions, where entire species are wiped out, are not unusual in our Earth's history.

zh: 这些包括第三次大规模灭绝，被称为'大灭绝'，它杀死了90%到96%的所有物种。
en: These include the third mass extinction, known as the 'Great Dying', which killed 90% to 96% of all species.

zh: 2015年，由美国布朗大学和杜克大学的科学家进行的一项研究，考察了物种因自然原因灭绝的速度。
en: A 2015 study by scientists who were based at Brown University and Duke University in the US, looked at how quickly species die out due to natural causes.

zh: 如果第六次大规模灭绝发生，研究过这个问题的科学家认为，地球上多达四分之三的物种可能会灭绝。
en: If a sixth mass extinction occurs, scientists who have studied the issue believe that up to three quarters of all species on Earth could die out.

zh: 我们需要采取措施拯救濒危物种，包括建立可以保护动植物的特殊区域。
en: We need to take steps to save endangered species, including setting up special areas where plants and animals can be protected.

zh: 今天早上，我花了40分钟去市中心。
en: This morning, it took me 40 minutes to go downtown.

zh: A10公路上有更多的道路施工！
en: More roadworks on the A10!

zh: 哦，真的吗？我花了一个多小时。
en: Oh really? It took me over an hour.

zh: M11公路上发生了一起事故。
en: There was an accident on the M11.

zh: 你多久听到一次这些对话？
en: How often have you heard these conversations?

zh: 我们多久被困在交通堵塞中？
en: How often do we get stuck in traffic jams?

zh: 我们多久会压力过大、疲惫和愤怒地到达工作或学校？
en: How often do we arrive at work or school, stressed out, tired and angry?

zh: 对许多人来说，答案是每天。
en: For many people, the answer is every day.

zh: 但与社会的真实成本相比，个人的愤怒和压力算不了什么。
en: But personal anger and stress are nothing compared to the real costs socially.

zh: 以下是一些关于英国的数据：
en: Here are some figures about Britain:

zh: 在过去的20年里，道路上的汽车数量增加了25%。
en: In the last 20 years, the number of cars on the roads has gone up by 25%.

zh: 现在有3200万辆汽车。
en: There are now 32 million cars.

zh: 在过去十年中，平均每年有2500人死于车辆事故。
en: Over the last decade, an average of 2,500 people have died every year in vehicle accidents.

zh: 交通是全球变暖和气候变化的主要原因之一，因为汽车发动机会产生各种类型的温室气体。
en: Traffic is one of the major causes of global warming and climate change, as car engines produce various types of greenhouse gases.

zh: 每年有近3万人死于空气污染。
en: Almost 30,000 deaths per year are caused by air pollution.

zh: 某些类型的癌症与交通污染有关。
en: Some types of cancer are related to traffic pollution.

zh: 我们知道汽车对我们有害。
en: We know that cars are bad for us.

zh: 但我们都会找借口：“公交车太糟糕了”；“火车总是晚点”；“我没时间走路”。
en: But we all make excuses: “The buses are terrible”; “The trains are always late”; “I haven't got time to walk”.

zh: 我也是在说我自己。
en: I'm talking about myself, too.

zh: 坦白说，我对我的车上瘾了。
en: To be frank, I'm addicted to my car.

zh: 当我向一位在绿色生活组织工作的朋友请教如何戒掉时，她告诉了我五件事：
en: When I asked a friend working for a green-living organisation for advice about how to give up, she told me five things:

zh: 2014年，超过55%的汽车行程在八公里以下。
en: In 2014, over 55% of car journeys were under eight kilometres.

zh: 我们可以轻松地步行或骑自行车，而不是进行短途汽车旅行。
en: Instead of taking short car journeys, we could easily walk or cycle.

zh: 腿部力量可以节省你的钱，保持健康，并帮助你更长寿。
en: Leg power can save your money, keep you fit and help you live longer.

zh: 定期锻炼还能将患心脏病的风险降低50%！
en: Regular exercise also cuts the risk of heart disease by 50%!

zh: 一辆公交车最多可容纳90人，而同样数量的人至少需要18辆汽车。
en: Up to 90 people can travel in one bus, while the same number will need at least 18 cars.

zh: 有时可能会多花一点时间，但那又怎样？
en: Sometimes it can take a little bit longer, but so what?

zh: 你可以放松一下，读一本书，和某人聊聊天——谁知道呢？
en: You can relax, read a book, talk to someone — who knows?

zh: 你真的必须去城镇另一边的那个购物中心吗？
en: Do you really have to go to that shopping centre on the other side of town?

zh: 拐角处的商店怎么样？
en: What about the shops around the corner?

zh: 在你上车之前，想一想你是否真的需要进行那次行程。
en: Before you get into your car, think about whether you really need to make that journey.

zh: 如果你真的必须使用汽车，就和别人共享行程。
en: If you really have to use a car, share journeys with someone else.

zh: 这样更便宜，也对环境更友好。
en: It is much cheaper and kinder to the environment.

zh: 我们常常认为对于交通的噪音、污染和危险，我们无能为力。
en: We often think there is nothing we can do about the noise, pollution and danger of traffic.

zh: 如果你的街道交通繁忙，就和你的邻居谈谈。
en: If your street is full of heavy traffic, talk to your neighbours about it.

zh: 去找市政府。
en: Go to the city government.

zh: 要求设置限速。
en: Ask for a speed limit.

zh: 做任何适合你的这些事情。
en: Do whichever of these things that suit you.

zh: 不要只是坐着抱怨！
en: Don't just sit around and complain!

zh: 都很简单，不是吗？
en: All quite simple, isn't it?

zh: 五个改善环境的简单方法。
en: Five easy ways to improve our environment.

zh: 好吧，很抱歉我必须结束这篇文章，但我下午4点必须去学校接我女儿。
en: Well, I'm sorry I have to finish this article, but I've got to pick up my daughter from school at 4 pm.

zh: 然后我必须开车去超市购物。
en: Then I've got to drive to the supermarket to do the shopping.

zh: 之后，我必须带我儿子去参加一个聚会。
en: After that, I have to take my son to a party.

zh: 交通状况会很糟糕，但我能做什么呢？
en: The traffic is going to be horrible, but what can I do?

zh: 工厂化养殖涉及将农场动物关在建筑物内以提高肉类或蛋类的产量。
en: Factory farming involves keeping farm animals inside buildings to increase the production of meat or eggs.

zh: 根据一家研究所的数据，“世界上74%的家禽、43%的牛肉和68%的鸡蛋是以这种方式生产的。”
en: According to an institute, "74% of the world’s poultry, 43% of its beef and 68% of its eggs are produced in this way."

zh: 支持工厂化养殖的主要论点是，除了比传统或有机养殖便宜得多之外，它还能为据联合国统计已达到七十亿的世界人口提供更多食物。
en: The main argument for factory farming is that, apart from being a lot cheaper than traditional or organic farming, it provides more food for a world population that has already reached seven billion, according to the United Nations.

zh: 由于工厂化农场不使用很多工人，因此成本低，产量高。
en: As factory farms do not use many workers, the costs are low and the production is high.

zh: 此外，工厂化养殖的动物比那些生活在户外的动物患病的几率更少。
en: Moreover, factory-farmed animals suffer from fewer diseases than those living outside.

zh: 另一方面，工厂化养殖的反对者说这对动物很残忍。
en: On the other hand, opponents of factory farming say that it is cruel to the animals.

zh: 例如，许多农场动物被关在几乎无法移动的小空间里。
en: For instance, many farm animals are kept in small spaces where they can hardly move.

zh: 为动物权利而战的人声称这些动物承受着巨大的压力。
en: People who fight for animal rights claim that these animals suffer terrible stress.

zh: 除此之外，工厂化养殖对环境有负面影响，因为它会产生大量废品，并使用大量化学品来控制昆虫和使用药物治疗疾病（英国今日农业，2013年）。
en: In addition to this, factory farming has a negative impact on the environment because it creates a lot of waste products, and uses a lot of chemicals to control insects and drugs to treat diseases (UK Farming Today, 2013).

zh: 总而言之，尽管生产廉价食品，但工厂化养殖对地球和动物本身都是有害的。
en: To sum up, despite producing cheap food, factory farming is bad for the planet and for the animals themselves.

zh: 在我看来，我们应该努力减少这种养殖方式，尽管我们将不得不为我们的鸡蛋和肉类支付更多的费用。
en: In my opinion, we should try to reduce this kind of farming, although we would have to pay more for our eggs and meat.

zh: 鸡为什么要过马路？我们可能永远也不会知道，因为她可能从未到达另一边。
en: Why did the chicken cross the road? We may never know since she's probably never got to the other side.

zh: 这段视频展示了动物在人类建造的环境中生活时面临的不同障碍，并提供了一些解决方案。
en: This video presents different barriers animals face when living in human-built environments, and also offers some solutions.

zh: “世界遗产地”的官方称号由联合国教育、科学及文化组织（UNESCO）委员会授予世界上那些在文化或自然历史方面具有杰出典范的地方。
en: The official title of "World Heritage Site" is given by the United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organisation (UNESCO) committee to places in the world that are outstanding examples of cultural or natural history.

zh: 截至2019年，中国有55处世界遗产，与意大利的数量相同。
en: Up to 2019, there are 55 world heritage sites in China, which has the same number of sites as Italy.

zh: 以下是其中的三处。
en: Here are three of them.

zh: 孔庙和孔府位于山东省曲阜市，是纪念孔子的场所。
en: The Temple and Mansion of Confucius is located in Qufu, Shandong Province, and is a memorial to Confucius.

zh: 地面上有900多个厅堂和房间。
en: On the ground, there are more than 900 halls and rooms.

zh: 人们参观最多的建筑是孔庙。
en: The building that people visit most is the Temple of Confucius.

zh: 除了庙宇，游客还可以欣赏外面的场地，那里有1000多块石碑和超过10万棵树。
en: Besides the temple, visitors can enjoy the grounds outside, where there are more than 1,000 stone tablets and over 100,000 trees.

zh: 丽江古城距离云南省大理古城有几小时的公交车程。
en: The Old Town of Lijiang is a few hours bus-ride away from the ancient city of Dali in Yunnan Province.

zh: 丽江古城被田野、山川和河流环绕，在春夏时节看起来像一块玉砚。
en: Surrounded by fields, mountains and rivers, the Old Town of Lijiang looks like a jade ink stone in spring and summer.

zh: 每年，许多外国游客参观这个地方，这个地方对他们来说就像“东方威尼斯”。
en: Each year, many foreign tourists visit the place and the place is like "Venice in the East" to them.

zh: 这里有狭窄的石巷、石桥以及带有雕刻门和彩绘窗户的房屋。
en: There are narrow stone alleys, stone bridges and houses with carved doors and painted windows.

zh: 九寨沟风景名胜区位于四川省北部，是一处独特的自然奇观。
en: Jiuzhaigou Valley Scenic and Historic Interest Area, north of Sichuan Province, is a unique natural wonder.

zh: 每年，来自世界各地的游客前来欣赏山脉、湖泊、溪流、树木、地下泉和瀑布，这些使得九寨沟成为一个具有杰出自然美的地区。
en: Every year, visitors from all over the world come to admire the mountains, lakes, streams, trees, underground springs and waterfalls, which make Jiuzhaigou Valley an area of outstanding natural beauty.

zh: 这个山谷是许多受保护植物物种和濒危动物的家园。
en: The valley is home to many protected species of plants and endangered animals.

zh: 在过去的几年里，我们目睹了大规模的环境灾难，专家预测更糟的情况将会到来。
en: In the last few years, we have seen environmental disasters on a grand scale, and experts are predicting worse to come.

zh: 金莉报道了我们地球多变的气候模式。
en: Jin Li reports on our Earth’s changeable weather patterns.

zh: 雷暴、洪水、地震、台风、火山喷发、海啸和森林火灾变得越来越常见。
en: Thunderstorms, floods, earthquakes, typhoons, volcanic eruptions, tsunamis, and forest fires have become increasingly common.

zh: 亚洲、非洲、美洲和大洋洲都发生了严重的洪水。
en: There have been terrible floods in Asia, Africa, America and Oceania.

zh: 各地的风暴也变得越来越严重，有更多的飓风袭击美国和中美洲。
en: Storms have been getting worse everywhere too, with more hurricanes hitting the US and Central America.

zh: 降雨量减少多年来一直影响着非洲，2017年索马里和肯尼亚发生了严重干旱，许多其他地区也变得更加干燥。
en: Reduced rainfall has affected Africa for years, with severe droughts in Somalia and Kenya in 2017, as well as many other zones becoming drier.

zh: 加拿大降雨量减少导致2016年5月发生了一场巨大的森林火灾。
en: Reduced rainfall in Canada resulted in a huge forest fire in May 2016.

zh: 火山喷发和地震一直是一个威胁。
en: Volcanic eruptions and earthquakes have always been a threat.

zh: 2016年，印度尼西亚几乎同时发生了三次火山喷发。
en: In 2016, there were three volcanic eruptions at almost the same time in Indonesia.

zh: 亚洲、欧洲和南美洲的许多地区都发生了严重的地震。
en: There have been serious earthquakes in many parts of Asia, Europe and South America.

zh: 2016年8月，一场大规模地震袭击了意大利中部，造成至少247人死亡。
en: A massive earthquake hit central Italy in August 2016, killing at least 247 people.

zh: 那么，为什么大自然开始与我们作对呢？
en: So why is nature beginning to turn on us?

zh: 世界人口正以每小时1万人、每年近9000万人的速度增长。
en: The population of the world is growing at the rate of 10,000 people an hour, nearly 90 million a year.

zh: 大部分增长发生在发展中国家。
en: Most of the growth is in the developing world.

zh: 农业地区的穷人经常搬到城市。
en: Poor people in agricultural areas often move to the cities.

zh: 他们用能找到的任何材料建造房屋。
en: They build homes from whatever materials they can find.

zh: 这些房屋在地震中很容易倒塌，或者在滑坡中滑下山坡，尤其是在大雨之后。
en: These homes can easily collapse during earthquakes or slip and slide downhill in landslides, especially after heavy rain.

zh: 除此之外，还有全球变暖的额外因素。
en: On top of that, there is the added aspect of global warming.

zh: 这主要是由工厂和车辆产生的大量二氧化碳造成的。
en: This has mainly been caused by the huge amounts of carbon dioxide produced by factories and vehicles.

zh: 世界森林的破坏也起到了一定作用。
en: The destruction of the world’s forests plays a part, too.

zh: 结果，更热的海洋导致更强的风。
en: As a result, a hotter ocean causes stronger winds.

zh: 现在大西洋的飓风比30年前强了40%。
en: Atlantic hurricanes are 40% stronger now than they were 30 years ago.

zh: 现在，滑坡和地震比过去更加危险，因为现在世界上大约一半的人口居住在城市。
en: Landslides and earthquakes are even more dangerous now than in the past because around half of the world’s population now live in cities.

zh: 南美洲和亚洲城镇中的许多人面临滑坡的风险。
en: Many people in cities and towns in South America and Asia are at risk from landslides.

zh: 2017年4月，哥伦比亚的莫科阿市遭遇滑坡，造成多人死亡和受伤。
en: In April 2017, the city of Mocoa in Colombia was hit by a landslide, leaving many dead and injured.

zh: 也有大量人口生活在地震的风险之中。
en: A great number of people live at risk from earthquakes too.

zh: 在过去的一百年里，这些（地震）已夺走了超过160万人的生命。
en: These have claimed more than 1.6 million lives in the last hundred years.

zh: 大多数灾害专家认为情况可能会变得更糟。
en: Most disaster experts believe that things could get a lot worse.

zh: 北京大学的侯明教授研究火山。
en: Professor Hou Ming of Peking University studies volcanoes.

zh: 他警告说，世界还没有……
en: He warns that the world has not...

zh: 人类历史上最严重的火山喷发可能是1815年印度尼西亚的坦博拉火山。
en: The worst eruption in human history was probably Mt Tambora in 1815, in Indonesia.

zh: 火山产生的尘埃席卷了地球的天空。
en: Dust from the volcano rolled across the Earth's skies.

zh: 第二年，欧洲和美洲都没有夏天。
en: There was no summer in Europe and America the following year.

zh: 但有证据表明，73,000年前发生过一次规模大得多的喷发。
en: But evidence shows that 73,000 years ago there was a much greater eruption.

zh: 它使一些地方的气温可能降低了6摄氏度，并使整个地球进入了持续数年的冬季。
en: It reduced temperatures by maybe 6°C in some places, and the whole planet was sent into winter for years.

zh: 而每10万年大约会发生两次这样的事件……
en: And there are about two of these events every 100,000 years...

zh: 世界上有成千上万的濒危物种，如果它们灭绝的速度持续上升，数百万的动植物将在未来十年内灭绝。
en: There are thousands of endangered species in the world and if the rate at which they are dying out continues to rise, millions of plants and animals will become extinct in the next decade.

zh: 大熊猫，作为保护的象征，可能是其中之一。
en: The Giant Panda, a symbol of conservation, might be one of them.

zh: 除了少数生活在世界各地的动物园和保护中心外，熊猫只在中国西南部的竹林中发现。
en: Apart from the few living in zoos and conservation centres around the world, pandas are found only in the bamboo forest in southwest China.

zh: 它们濒临灭绝，因为它们的栖息地由于人类活动正在被破坏。
en: They are endangered as their habitats are being destroyed due to human activities.

zh: 根据2015年的一项调查，由于保护努力，熊猫的数量正在增加。
en: Because of conservation efforts, the number of pandas is increasing according to a survey in 2015.

zh: 这个消息令人兴奋，但要拯救它免于灭绝，还有很多工作要做。
en: This news is exciting, but much still has to be done to save it from extinction.

zh: 长江豚是中国另一种面临严重威胁的物种。
en: The Yangtze Dolphin is another species under severe threat in China.

zh: 由于河流污染、非法狩猎和其他人类活动，它们的数量正在减少。
en: The number of them is decreasing due to pollution in the river, illegal hunting and other human activities.

zh: 总结我的演讲，我想提醒大家，动物在我们的世界里没有发言权。
en: To sum up my presentation, I want to remind everyone that animals have no voice in our world.

zh: 我们需要在它们永远消失之前为它们发声。
en: We need to speak out on their behalf before they disappear for good.

zh: 我最近从海滩旅行回来，看到海滩和水中有大量的塑料，感到非常沮丧。
en: I recently came back from a trip to the beach and was very upset to see the amount of plastic on the beach and in the water.

zh: 看到环境被这样破坏，令人失望。
en: It's disappointing to see the environment destroyed like this.

zh: 尽管许多人试图回收，但不幸的是，仍然有如此多的塑料废物最终被扔掉。
en: Even though many people are trying to recycle, there is unfortunately still so much plastic waste that ends up being thrown away.

zh: 然后我考虑做点什么。
en: Then I thought about doing something.

zh: 因此，我发明了一种新型包装。
en: As a result, I invented a new type of packaging.

zh: 它由植物叶子制成，和塑料一样轻。
en: It is made from plant leaves and it is as light as plastic.

zh: 它可以制成袋子或其他类型的形状。
en: It can be made into either bags or other types of shape.

zh: 它非常方便消费者使用。
en: It is very convenient for consumers to use.

zh: 我很高兴我因这项环保创新赢得了奖项。
en: I am so happy that I have won an award for this innovation for the environment.

zh: 我希望每个人都能为环境做点什么。
en: I hope that everyone can do something for the environment.

zh: 很多人觉得开私家车能让他们拥有更多自由，他们经常抱怨公共交通的不便。
en: A lot of people feel that driving their own cars allows them to have more freedom and they often complain about the inconvenience of public transport.

zh: 嗯，在公共交通上的等待时间、拥挤和出汗确实让人感到烦恼，但为了保护环境，我认为使用公共交通是值得付出的努力。
en: Well, waiting time, crowds and sweating on public transport do leave people feeling annoyed, but in order to protect the environment, I think using public transport is worth the effort.

zh: 首先，研究表明，在一些大城市，约90%的总污染物来自汽车。
en: First of all, research shows that around 90% of total pollutants in some big cities come from cars.

zh: 这些污染物对环境有害，对我们的健康极其危险。
en: These pollutants are harmful to the environment and extremely dangerous to our health.

zh: 它们会损害我们的肺部，并导致诸如癌症等疾病。
en: They can damage our lungs and cause illnesses such as cancer.

zh: 过度依赖汽车还会造成诸如交通堵塞、交通事故和噪音污染等问题。
en: Relying too much on cars also creates problems such as traffic jams, road accidents and noise pollution.

zh: 当然，汽车在长途旅行时是方便且必要的，但对于城市内的短途旅行，骑自行车通常更快。
en: Of course, cars are convenient and necessary when travelling long distances, but for short journeys within the city it is often faster to cycle.

zh: 所以让我们现在就采取行动吧。
en: So let us take action now.

zh: 成为一名海洋研究员是我一直梦寐以求的事情。
en: Being a marine researcher is something that I've always dreamed of.

zh: 我在一个国际项目中与一队科学家合作，他们都是各自领域的专家。
en: I work with a team of scientists, who are all experts in their fields, on an international project.

zh: 我目前常驻阿根廷，位于南美洲，研究来自南极洲的海水温度。
en: I'm currently based in Argentina, which is in South America, studying sea temperatures from Antarctica.

zh: 不幸的是，过去50年来温度显著上升，这显然与人类活动有关。
en: Unfortunately, there has been a significant rise in temperatures over the past 50 years, which is clearly linked to human activity.

zh: 海水变暖的影响包括更频繁的风暴、自然灾害，以及最令人担忧的冰盖融化。
en: The effects of hotter seas include more frequent storms, natural disasters and, most worryingly of all, the melting of the ice caps.

zh: 即使在十年前还全年冻结的极地冰盖，现在正以惊人的速度融化。
en: Polar ice caps that were frozen all year long, even ten years ago are now melting at an alarming rate.

zh: 我希望通过教育人们了解这一点并分享研究，我们能作为一个全球社区共同努力保护我们的星球。
en: I hope by educating people about this and sharing research we can work together as a global community to protect our planet.

zh: 为什么不打包一个帐篷呢？我们可以在我知道的一个美丽森林附近露营过夜。
en: Why not pack a tent? We can camp overnight near a beautiful forest I know of.

zh: 你下周想和我一起骑自行车吗？我妈妈可以开车送我们去山里，我们可以从那里开始。
en: Would you like to join me for a cycle next week? My mother can drive us to the mountain and we can start from there.

zh: 太好了。我也有一个帐篷，但这次我们可以用你的。
en: Great. I have a tent too but we can use yours this time.

zh: 我们为什么不干脆骑自行车呢？这对我们的健康和环境都会更好。
en: Why don't we just cycle instead? It would be better for our health and the environment.

zh: 太棒了。我等不及要去了！我已经好久没有骑自行车或露营了。
en: Wonderful. I can't wait to go! I haven't cycled or camped for ages.

zh: 玛蒂尔德·卢瓦泽尔，一个美丽的女人，对她的生活感到痛苦。
en: Mathilde Loisel, a beautiful woman is miserable with her life.

zh: 尽管出身于一个职员家庭，她总觉得自己“从更高的地位跌落”，并渴望成为上流社会的一员。
en: Although from a family of clerks, she always feels she has "fallen from a higher station” and longs to be part of the upper class.

zh: 由于没有实现这些梦想的前景，她嫁给了一个中产阶级的巴黎职员。
en: With no prospect of meeting these dreams she marries a middle-class Parisian clerk.

zh: 这让她很不快乐，忍受着她认为的贫困生活。
en: This leaves her unhappy, suffering from what she thinks as a life of poverty.

zh: 尽管她丈夫的收入足以满足他们的需求，玛蒂尔德仍然梦想着变得富有。
en: Although her husband's income is sufficient for their needs, Mathilde still dreams of being wealthy.

zh: 玛蒂尔德专注于她缺乏诸如礼服和珠宝之类的财产，而不是享受她现有的生活。
en: Mathilde focuses on her lack of possessions such as gowns and jewellery instead of enjoying the life she has.

zh: 她甚至嫉妒她的校友福雷斯蒂埃夫人，后者嫁给了一个有钱人。
en: She even becomes jealous of her school friend, Madame Forestier, who has married a wealthy man.

zh: 一天晚上，得意洋洋的卢瓦泽尔先生回到家，递给她一张在他工作的公共教育部举办的舞会的请柬。
en: One evening, the triumphant’ Monsieur Loisel comes home and hands her an invitation to a ball at the Ministry of Public Instruction where he works.

zh: 玛蒂尔德拒绝前往并将请柬扔到地上，这让他感到惊讶。
en: Mathilde surprises him by refusing to go and throws the invitation onto the floor.

zh: 她变得心烦意乱，并告诉她的丈夫，没有新礼服和珠宝，她不可能参加舞会。
en: She gets upset and tells her husband that without a new gown and jewellery she cannot possibly attend the ball.

zh: 卢瓦泽尔先生不情愿地同意支付一件价值四百法郎的礼服，他知道他将无法负担他一直攒钱想买的猎枪。
en: Monsieur Loisel reluctantly agrees to pay for a gown costing four hundred francs, knowing he will not be able to afford the hunting gun he had been saving up to buy.

zh: 玛蒂尔德买了裙子，但抱怨说她仍然没有珠宝。
en: Mathilde buys the dress but complains that she still has no jewellery.

zh: 不过这一次，当她的丈夫想出向她的朋友借一些珠宝的解决办法时，玛蒂尔德很高兴。
en: For once though, Mathilde is pleased when her husband comes up with the solution to ask her friend to lend some jewellery,

zh: 福雷斯蒂埃夫人让玛蒂尔德选择她想要的，玛蒂尔德从一个黑色缎面盒子里挑选了一条美丽的钻石项链。
en: Madame Forestier offers Mathilde to choose what she wants and Mathilde selects a beautiful diamond necklace from a black satin box.

zh: 当福雷斯蒂埃夫人看到这条项链让她的朋友感到多么高兴时，她同意把它借给她去参加舞会。
en: When Madame Forestier sees how happy the necklace makes her friend feel she agrees to lend it to her for the ball.

zh: 在舞会上，玛蒂尔德是那里最优雅、最端庄的女人，微笑着，很快乐。
en: At the ball, Mathilde is the most elegant and graceful woman there, smiling and happy.

zh: 所有的男人都问她的名字，并想被介绍给她。
en: All the men ask her name and want to be introduced to her.

zh: 她和内阁的所有随员跳舞，甚至被部长注意到。
en: She dances with all of the attachés from the cabinet and is even noticed by the minister.

zh: 带着幸福和激情跳舞，陶醉在喜悦中，玛蒂尔德暂时存在于她渴望成为其中一员的世界里。
en: Dancing with happiness and passion, intoxicated with pleasure, Mathilde exists for a time in the world she longs to be part of.

zh: 凌晨四点，她准备离开。
en: At 4am, she is ready to leave.

zh: 当卢瓦泽尔先生把披肩披在妻子肩上时，她寒酸的披肩与优雅的舞会礼服形成的对比令人难以忍受，为了避免尴尬，她急忙在任何人注意到她之前离开。
en: As Monsieur Loisel places the wrap over his wife's shoulders, the contrast of the poverty of her wrap with the elegant ball gown is too much to bear and to save any embarrassment she hurries to leave before anyone notices her.

zh: 当他们到达街上时，他们找不到马车，于是开始步行回家。
en: When they reach the street they cannot find a cab and start to walk home.

zh: 他们绝望地朝塞纳河走去，冷得发抖。
en: They walk towards the Seine in despair, shivering with cold.

zh: 最后在码头边，他们找到了“那种古老的黑夜马车，它们仿佛羞于在白天显露其破旧，天黑以后才在巴黎周围出现”。
en: At last on the quay side they find "one of those ancient night cabs which, as though they were ashamed to show their shabbiness during the day, are never seen round Paris until after dark".

zh: 卢瓦泽尔夫妇回到了殉道者街的家，当玛蒂尔德照镜子再次欣赏自己时，发现项链不见了，她的快乐变成了恐惧。
en: The Loisels arrive home at the Rue des Martyrs, and Mathilde's happiness turns to horror as she sees that the necklace is missing when she looks in the mirror to admire herself one more time.

zh: 她和卢瓦泽尔先生疯狂地寻找，但他们
en: She and Monsieur Loisel search frantically, but they

zh: 卢瓦泽尔先生甚至沿着整个路线重走了他们的脚步，然后第二天花时间询问并试图找到项链。
en: Monsieur Loisel even retraces their steps back along the whole route and then spends the next day enquiring and trying to find the necklace.

zh: 最后，在失败中，他建议玛蒂尔德给福雷斯蒂埃夫人写一封信，解释说项链会在修理后归还，谎称搭扣坏了。
en: Finally, in defeat, he suggests Mathilde writes a letter to Madame Forestier, explaining she will return the necklace after repair, lying that the clasp had broken.

zh: 这个故事给了他们时间继续寻找。
en: This story allows them time to continue the search.

zh: 当卢瓦泽尔夫妇找不到项链时，他们用它的珠宝盒寻找购买它的珠宝商。
en: When the Loisels are unable to find the necklace, they use its jewellery box to search for the jeweller where it was purchased.

zh: 珠宝商向这对夫妇提供了一条类似的项链，价格为三万六千法郎，条件是如果他们能在二月底前找到项链，他会买回它。
en: The jeweller offers the couple a similar necklace for thirty-six-thousand francs with the understanding that he will buy it back if they find the necklace before the end of February.

zh: 然而，他们没有找到项链，并承担了巨大的债务，这永远改变了他们的生活。
en: However, they do not find the necklace, and they take on huge debt that forever changes their lives.

zh: 卢瓦泽尔先生被即将到来的麻烦、即将降临在他身上的“黑色苦难”、以及所有身体牺牲和“道德折磨”的前景所吓倒，去支付珠宝商三万六千法郎并取回钻石项链。
en: Monsieur Loisel, frightened by the trouble yet to come, by the “black misery” that is about to fall upon him, by the prospect of all the physical sacrifices and the "moral tortures" he is about to suffer, goes to pay the jeweller thirty-six-thousand francs and collects the diamond necklace.

zh: 当玛蒂尔德把新买的项链带给福雷斯蒂埃夫人时，她担心朋友会发现项链是替代品，并认为她是小偷。
en: When Mathilde takes the newly purchased necklace to Madame Forestier, she fears her friend will discover that the necklace is a replacement and consider her a thief.

zh: 尽管福雷斯蒂埃夫人批评玛蒂尔德归还项链晚了，但她从未打开盒子看它。
en: Although Madame Forestier criticises Mathilde for bringing the necklace back late, she never opens the case to look at it.

zh: 接下来的几年对玛蒂尔德来说是可怕的，她像仆人一样工作，她自己的仆人被解雇了。
en: The next years are horrible for Mathilde, who works like a servant, her own servant having been dismissed.

zh: 卢瓦泽尔夫妇搬到了更便宜的住房。
en: The Loisels move to cheaper housing.

zh: 玛蒂尔德穿着工作服，负责做所有家庭“十分讨厌的”家务活。
en: Mathilde dresses in work clothing and becomes responsible for doing all the family's “odious*” housework duties.

zh: 她以“突然的英雄主义”承担了这个角色。
en: She takes on this role with "sudden heroism".

zh: 卢瓦泽尔先生晚上做第二份工作。
en: Monsieur Loisel works a second job at night.

zh: 他们工作了十年来偿还债务。
en: They work for ten years to repay their debts.

zh: 剥夺的压力造成了损失，玛蒂尔德迅速衰老。
en: The strain of deprivation takes its toll, and Mathilde ages rapidly.

zh: 偶尔，她会回想并记住舞会的奇迹，但最终，他们的债务全部还清。
en: Occasionally, she thinks back and remembers the wonders of the ball, but finally, their debt is paid in full.

zh: 一天在街上，玛蒂尔德遇到福雷斯蒂埃夫人，她仍然看起来很可爱。
en: One day on the street, Mathilde meets Madame Forestier, still looking lovely.

zh: 起初没有认出玛蒂尔德，福雷斯蒂埃夫人被她朋友憔悴的外表震惊了。
en: At first not recognising Mathilde, Madam Forestier is shocked by her friend's haggard? appearance.

zh: 玛蒂尔德解释说，她的生活因为福雷斯蒂埃夫人而艰难。
en: Mathilde explains that her life has been hard because of Madame Forestier.

zh: 玛蒂尔德分享了关于她丢失和替换所借项链的真相。
en: Mathilde shares the truth regarding her loss and replacement of the necklace that she had borrowed.

zh: 她解释说，这是用十年的艰苦劳动购买的。
en: She explains that it was purchased with ten years of hard labour.

zh: 她自豪地描述了她如何履行对福雷斯蒂埃夫人和社会的义务。
en: She proudly describes how she met her obligation both to Madame Forestier and to society.

zh: 福雷斯蒂埃夫人深受感动，握住玛蒂尔德的手，告诉她真相。
en: Madame Forestier, deeply moved, takes Mathilde’s hands in her own and tells her the truth.

zh: 她借给玛蒂尔德的项链仅仅是价值仅五百法郎的 costume jewellery（仿制珠宝）。
en: The necklace that she had loaned Mathilde was mere costume jewellery worth only five hundred francs.

zh: 去年我开始教科学时，并不是很成功。
en: When I started teaching science last year, I wasn't very successful.

zh: 从学生的行为中我能看出，他们觉得我的课相当无聊。
en: I could tell from my students’ behaviour that they found my lessons quite boring.

zh: 他们会打哈欠，并且很难集中注意力。
en: They would yawn and struggle to pay attention.

zh: 我感到压力很大，但后来我意识到我需要做出改变。
en: I felt quite a lot of stress but then I realised that I needed to make a change.

zh: 之前，我用了很多难懂的科学术语，所以我改变了这一点并简化了语言。
en: Previously, I had used a lot of difficult scientific language so I changed this and simplified it.

zh: 我还决定使用更多实际例子和实验，帮助学生看到我所讲的内容，而不仅仅是听。
