en: I also decided to use a lot more practical examples and experiments to help the students see what I was talking about instead of just listening.

zh: 我还意识到他们很多人缺乏自信，因此当我要求他们在全班面前做展示时，他们很讨厌这样做，所以我改变了方法，加入了更多小组活动。
en: I also realised that many of them lack confidence and therefore hated it when I asked them to make a presentation in front of the class, so I changed my approach to involving more group work.

zh: 幸运的是，这个方法奏效了，现在我的学生告诉我他们喜欢我的课。
en: Fortunately, it worked and now my students tell me they love my classes.

zh: 彼得：嗨，珍妮，怎么了？你看起来很难过！
en: Peter: Hi, Jenny, what's wrong? You look miserable!

zh: 珍妮：哦，嗨，彼得。我刚从史密斯老师那里得到了一些关于我英语作文的非常负面的反馈。
en: Jenny: Oh, hi, Peter. I just got some very negative feedback from Ms Smith about my English essay.

zh: 她说文章的结构不是很清晰，所以很难理解，而且我用的例子不够具体。
en: She said the structure of it wasn't very clear so it was hard to follow and the examples I used were not specific enough.

zh: 听到这些，我的自信心受到了很大打击。
en: It just hurts my confidence so much to hear that.

zh: 彼得：我不会太担心，珍妮。
en: Peter: I wouldn't worry too much, Jenny.

zh: 虽然你认为这是负面的，但我认为史密斯老师认为这是直接的反馈。
en: Although you see it as negative, I think Ms Smith sees it as direct feedback.

zh: 她认为最好告诉你她确切的想法。
en: She believes it is better to tell you exactly what she thinks.

zh: 她对每个人都是这样，所以这不是针对个人的。
en: She's like that with everyone so it isn't personal.

zh: 我想我只是更喜欢更有支持性的反馈。
en: I guess my preference is just for more supportive feedback.

zh: 每个人，都曾一度或多次在友谊和与家人的关系中经历过一些挑战。
en: Everyone, at one time or another, has experienced some challenges in friendships and relationships with family members.

zh: 我们可能会发现自己对他人感到沮丧或愤怒，甚至发现我们会与他们争吵。
en: We might find ourselves frustrated or angry with other people, or even find that we argue with them.

zh: 现实是没有人是完美的，我们需要意识到我们应该找到方法过上更快乐、压力更小的生活。
en: The reality is that nobody is perfect and we need to realise that we should find ways to live happier and less stressful lives.

zh: 以下是一些关于如何让关系更快乐、更健康的建议。
en: Here are some tips on how to make relationships happier and healthier.

zh: 尊重他人并接纳他们。
en: Respect other people and accept them.

zh: 这是最重要的一点。
en: This is the most important point.

zh: 如果我们想向某人表达我们的爱，我们首先需要尊重他们是谁，并向他们表明我们接纳他们本身的样子。
en: If we want to show someone we love them, we need to first respect who they are and show them we accept them for who they are.

zh: 每个人都是独特的，有着不同的经历和生活。
en: Everyone is unique with different experiences and lives.

zh: 通过时刻牢记这一点，我们将能够发展和维持牢固的关系。
en: By always remembering this, we will be able to develop and maintain strong relationships.

zh: 我们可能有痴迷于运动的朋友，而我们更喜欢阅读。
en: We might have friends who are crazy about sports, while we prefer reading.

zh: 或者也许父母的爱好在我们看来很无聊，但那是他们喜爱的东西。
en: Or perhaps a parent's hobby seems boring to us but it is something they love.

zh: 如果我们想保持我们的关系牢固而积极，我们至少应该花时间倾听他们，并谈论对他们重要的事情。
en: If we want to keep our relationships strong and positive, we should at least take time to listen to them and talk about what matters to them.

zh: 通过这样做，我们向他们表明我们关心他们和他们的兴趣。
en: By doing this, we show them that we care about them and their interests.

zh: 这对我们大多数人来说是最难做的事情，然而一句简单的“对不起”可以消除很多紧张情绪。
en: This is the hardest thing for most of us to do, yet a simple “I’m sorry” can undo a lot of tension.

zh: 当犯错时保持谦逊，你可以解决你可能造成的任何问题，同时也表明你是一个成熟的人。
en: By being humble when you make a mistake, you can fix any problem you may have caused and also show that you are a mature person.

zh: 所以，尝试并遵循这些建议，你会发现你与朋友和所爱的人拥有更幸福、更牢固的关系。
en: So, try and follow the advice and you will find that you have happier and stronger relationships with your friends and loved ones.

zh: 当他们受伤时，他们会被治疗并休息一段时间，但他们会回来。
en: when they are injured, and they will be treated and have some time off but they will come back.

zh: 不知何故，他们觉得有趣的是，在许多世界比赛中，他们的竞争对手不是来自其他国家，而实际上是他们的队友。
en: Somehow the funny part they feel is that in a lot of world games their competitors are not from other countries but are actually their teammates.

zh: 但真正让中国队与众不同的是他们的荣誉感和决心，以维护他们国家队伍保持领先的传统。
en: But what really sets the Chinese team apart is their honour and determination to uphold the tradition of keeping their country's team in the lead.

zh: 嗨，杰夫。你在读什么？
en: Hi, Jeff. What are you reading?

zh: 嗨，亚历克斯。这是一本关于对成功的不同态度的有趣的书。
en: Hi, Alex. It's an interesting book about different attitudes towards success.

zh: 似乎成功是一个非常主观的概念。
en: It seems that success is a very subjective concept.

zh: 你这是什么意思？
en: What do you mean by that?

zh: 嗯，我的意思是成功对于不同的人来说是不同的。
en: Well, I mean success is different for different people.

zh: 如果你的抱负是成为一名百万富翁，那么你的银行账户就是最重要的事情。
en: If your ambition is to be a millionaire, then your bank account is the most important thing.

zh: 所以，如果家庭对你来说是最重要的事情，也许找到一个丈夫或妻子并拥有孩子就是你理解的成功。
en: So, if family is the most important thing for you, maybe finding a husband or wife and having children is what you would understand success to be.

zh: 看到这里各种各样的想法很有趣。
en: It's interesting to see the variety of ideas here.

zh: 那么，对你来说，未来的成功生活会是什么样子？
en: So, what would a successful life be like for you in the future?

zh: 我想如果我能够拥有一个幸福的家庭和一份科学事业，我就会觉得我已经取得了成功。
en: I think if I could have a happy family and a career in science I would feel I have achieved success.

zh: 对我来说，环游世界并且拥有一个幸福的家庭会让我感到快乐和成功。
en: For me, travelling around the world and also having a happy family would make me feel happy and successful.

zh: 一天，萨姆在网上读到一篇关于一位名叫托马斯·奥康纳的年轻科技亿万富翁的文章，一切都改变了。
en: One day, while reading an article online about a young technology billionaire called Thomas O’Connor, everything changed for Sam.

zh: 文章中，这位首席执行官说他的公司正试图创造一种新的、改进的在线支付商品的方式，但他们在安全系统方面遇到了问题。
en: In the article, the CEO said that his company was trying to create a new and improved way of paying for goods online but that they were having a problem with the security system.

zh: 他说，如果他们能克服这个问题，他们就能推出世界上最大、最好的在线支付系统。
en: He said if they could overcome this, they could launch the biggest and best online payment system in the world.

zh: 萨姆意识到他有一个如何解决这个问题的想法。
en: Sam realised he had an idea on how to solve this problem.

zh: 想着这不会有什么结果，他给首席执行官发了邮件，介绍了自己并解释了他的想法。
en: Thinking that it wouldn’t come to anything, he emailed the CEO, introducing himself and explaining his idea.

zh: 他完全忘记了这件事，继续他的生活和学习，但一个月后，最令人惊奇的事情发生了。
en: He forgot all about it and got on with his life and studies, but then a month later, the most amazing thing happened.

zh: 他直接收到了首席执行官的回复。
en: He got a response directly from the CEO.

zh: 他告诉他，他很喜欢他的想法，并希望他能飞到美国与他见面。
en: He told him he loved his idea and would like him to fly to America to meet with him.

zh: 他说他知道萨姆还需要完成学业，但当他完成学业后，他会很乐意给萨姆一份公司的工作。
en: He said he knew Sam still needed to finish school, but that when he had, he would be happy to offer Sam a job in the company.

zh: 萨姆欣喜若狂，但很快开始担心起来。
en: Sam was overjoyed, but soon began to worry.

zh: 他父母对他的抱负怎么办？
en: What about his parents’ ambition for him?

zh: 他应该告诉他们——还是拒绝这个提议？
en: Should he tell them-or turn down the offer?

zh: 这是一个艰难的决定。
en: It was a difficult decision to make.

zh: 在我们星球的历史进程中，已经发生过几次大规模灭绝事件。
en: Several mass extinction events have occurred in the course of our planet's history.

zh: 科学家们说，以前各种类型的物种正在被消灭，但人类活动将归咎于已经开始发生的第六次灭绝。
en: Scientists say that previously various types of species are being wiped out but human activity will be to blame for the sixth extinction which has already started to happen.

zh: 很明显，人类行为对地球上几乎所有的自然事物都产生了负面影响，从不同种类的植物和动物到产生温室气体。
en: It is clear that human actions negatively affects almost every natural thing on Earth, from different species of plants and animals to creating greenhouse gases.

zh: 事实上，物种现在被消灭的速度比历史上任何时候都要快。
en: In fact, species are being wiped out at a faster rate now than anytime in history.

zh: 根据科学家的说法，我们现在正面临着对地球造成无法修复的损害的风险。
en: According to scientists, we are now at the risk of causing damage to the planet that cannot be repaired.

zh: 想想路上的汽车数量。
en: Think of the amount of cars on the road.

zh: 现在我们知道癌症与交通污染有关，这就是为什么越来越多的人生病。
en: Now we know that cancer is related to traffic pollution, which is why more and more people are becoming sick.

zh: 我们作为年轻人必须为地球的未来而战，并呼吁每个人尊重地球。
en: We as young people must fight for our planet's future and call upon everyone to respect the Earth.

zh: 早上好，玛丽。你是怎么总是能比我先到办公室的？
en: Good morning, Mary. How do you always manage to the office before me?

zh: 早上好，马丁。嗯，我骑自行车，所以我不会堵在路上。
en: Morning, Martin. Well, I cycle so I don't get stuck in traffic.

zh: 嗯，我不骑自行车，但我确实乘坐公共交通工具。
en: Well, I don't cycle but I do take public transport.

zh: 它没有骑自行车那么快，但比我开车能更快地到达市中心。
en: It's not quite as fast as cycling but it gets me downtown much quicker than if I take my car.

zh: 公共交通也是帮助减少大气中温室气体水平的好方法。
en: Public transport is also a good way to help reduce the level of greenhouse gases in our atmosphere.

zh: 如果更多的人乘坐公共汽车和火车，对我们所有人都会更好。
en: If more people take the bus and train, it would be better for us all.

zh: 我认识很多人抱怨交通堵塞和空气污染，但他们无法解决这些问题。
en: I know so many people who complain about traffic jam and air pollution, but they cannot solve the problems.

zh: 这种态度很普遍，但我认为越来越多的人开始做一些适合他们并保护环境的事情。
en: That kind of attitude is common but I think more and more people are beginning to do something which suits them and protect the environment.

zh: 当今世界，许多动物和植物都濒临灭绝。
en: Many animals and plants are endangered in the world today.

zh: 这些濒危物种面临着灭绝的威胁，这意味着它们将不再存在于地球上。
en: These endangered species are threatened with becoming extinct, meaning they will no longer exist on Earth.

zh: 例子包括古巴金刚鹦鹉和斯里兰卡豆科树。
en: Examples include the Cuban Macaw and the Sri Lankan Legume Tree.

zh: 狩猎和收集金刚鹦鹉作为宠物导致了它的灭绝。
en: Hunting, and collecting of the Macaw bird for pets, led to its extinction.

zh: 而本土的斯里兰卡豆科树灭绝的主要原因是栖息地的丧失。
en: Whilst the main reason the native Sri Lankan Legume Tree went extinct was due to habitat loss.

zh: 动植物消失的主要原因通常是由于狩猎、栖息地丧失甚至入侵物种的引入对食物链造成的破坏。
en: The main cause for animals and plants disappearing is often a disruption to the food chain due to hunting, habitat loss or even the introduction of invasive species.

zh: 从单细胞动物到蓝鲸，每一种生物都需要进食。
en: Every living thing from one-celled animals to a blue whale needs to eat.

zh: 自然由许多脆弱的食物链连接和控制。
en: Nature is connected and controlled by many fragile food chains.

zh: 食物链描述了在一个栖息地中谁吃谁。
en: A food chain describes who eats whom in a habitat.

zh: 当食物链中的一个环节不再存在——例如，一个物种灭绝——食物链就会断裂，有时这会导致其他动物消失，整个系统可能变得不平衡甚至崩溃。
en: When one of the links in a food chain is no longer present — for example, a species goes extinct — the food chain breaks and sometimes this can cause other animals to disappear and the whole system can become imbalanced or even collapsed.

zh: 人类可能对食物链产生灾难性的影响。
en: Humans can have disastrous effects on food chains.

zh: 当人们最初探索世界时，他们将动植物物种从他们的祖国带到了他们探索和定居的地方。
en: When people first explored the world, they took animal and plant species from their home countries to the places they explored and settled in.

zh: 他们没有意识到引入入侵物种的后果。
en: They did not realise the consequences of introducing invasive species.

zh: 通过这样做，他们破坏了所探索地区的自然食物链。
en: By doing so, they were disrupting the natural food chains of the areas they explored.

zh: 如今，有严格的规则控制着动植物在国家间的流动。
en: Nowadays there are strict rules controlling the movement of animals and plants between countries.

zh: 但世界上一些地区仍然在经历数百年前引入的入侵物种所带来的问题。
en: But some parts of the world are still experiencing problems with invasive species introduced hundreds of years ago.

zh: 随着对我们如何影响自然环境的意识不断提高，我们有希望学会保护这些食物链并帮助它们繁荣发展。
en: With rising awareness of how we affect the natural environment, hopefully we can learn to protect these food chains and help them to thrive.

zh: 否则，物种的持续丧失最终将意味着我们自身的灭绝。
en: Otherwise the continued loss of species will eventually mean our own extinction.

zh: 过去完成时表示在过去的事件之前发生的事件或存在的状态。
en: We use the past perfect to talk about an event or situation in the past which happened before other past events.

zh: 警察逮捕了那个闯入珠宝店的人。
en: The police arrested the man who had broken into a jewellery shop.

zh: 过去完成时和一般过去时用在同一个句子中体现过去事件发生的顺序。
en: We use the past perfect and the past simple in one sentence to show the order of past events.

zh: 我到达时她离开了。
en: When I arrived, she left.

zh: 我到达时她已经离开了。
en: When I arrived, she had left.

zh: 过去完成时的构成是: 主语 + had + 动词 -ed 形式。
en: The past perfect is formed in this way: subject + had + verb-ed.

zh: 我以前见过他。
en: I had met him before.

zh: 汤姆在其他人开始之前就开始他的项目了吗？
en: Had Tom begun his project before anyone else did?

zh: 他们之前没有移动他们的车。
en: They hadn't moved their car earlier.

zh: 动词 -ing 形式在句中起名词作用。
en: A verb-ing form acts like a noun in a sentence.

zh: 可以用作主语、表语和宾语 (在动词或介词之后)。
en: We can use the verb-ing form as subject, predicative and object (after a verb or a preposition).

zh: 吸烟是一个坏习惯。
en: Smoking is a terrible habit.

zh: 产品和服务的广告是大生意。
en: The advertising of products and services is big business.

zh: 他的爱好是集邮。
en: His hobby is collecting stamps.

zh: 他不喜欢我唱歌。
en: He doesn't like my singing.

zh: 我们因工作一整天而感到疲倦。
en: We are tired from working all day.

zh: 不要混淆作为介词的 to 和动词不定式的 to。
en: Do not confuse to as a preposition with to in the to-infinitive.

zh: 动词 -ing 形式用于介词 to 之后。
en: The verb-ing form is used after the preposition to.

zh: 吉姆期待着收到你的回复。
en: Jim is looking forward to getting your reply.

zh: 我不在的时候你得喂猫。
en: You have to feed the cat when I'm away.

zh: 我认为猫是最优雅的动物。
en: I think the cat is the most graceful animal.

zh: 无家可归者受到慈善组织的帮助。
en: The homeless are helped by charity organisations.

zh: 年轻人的想法与他们的父母不同。
en: The young think differently from their parents.

zh: 定语从句有两种：限制性定语从句和非限制性定语从句。
en: There are two kinds of relative clauses: defining clauses and non-defining clauses.

zh: 限制性定语从句修饰名词或代词。
en: Defining clauses identify nouns or pronouns.

zh: 表明所修饰的是哪个人或物。
en: They make clear which person or thing, etc. is being referred to.

zh: 正在打网球的女孩是我的朋友。
en: The girl who is playing tennis is my friend.

zh: 这是我藏钥匙的地方。
en: This is the place where I hid the key.

zh: 她仍然记得她注意到汽车丢失的那个时候。
en: She still remembers the time when she noticed the car was missing.

zh: 他们是那些房子着火的人。
en: They are the people whose houses caught fire.

zh: 非限制性定语从句提供句子意义以外的额外信息。
en: Non-defining relative clauses give extra information which is not necessary to the meaning of the sentence.

zh: 我的朋友来自埃塞俄比亚，他正在打网球。
en: My friend, who is playing tennis, comes from Ethiopia.

zh: 这起事故发生在昨天，已导致三人死亡。
en: The accident, which happened yesterday, has led to three deaths.

zh: 这栋建筑位于市中心，一个重要的会议正在那里举行。
en: The building, where an important conference is being held, is in the centre of the city.

zh: 教授给了我很多建议，我从中受益匪浅。
en: The professor gave me a lot of advice, from which I benefited a lot.

zh: 在非限制性定语从句中，关系代词which可以指代整个句子。
en: In non-defining clauses, the relative pronoun which can refer to an entire sentence.

zh: 雪下得很大，这让孩子们感到兴奋。
en: It is snowing heavily, which makes children feel excited.

zh: 这条河被严重污染了，这引起了公众的广泛关注。
en: The river is seriously polluted, which has caused much public attention.

zh: 悲伤的是，两年前她去世了，但我一直很想念她。
en: Sadly, she passed away two years ago but I still think of her all the time.

zh: 海内存知己，天涯若比邻。
en: A bosom friend afar brings a distant land near.

zh: 我唯一还记得的数学课内容是三角形的内角和是180度。
en: The only thing I can remember from school maths is that the angles of a triangle add up to 180 degrees!

zh: 有一次我撞见他和几个朋友比赛，看谁能从学校的舞台上跳得最远。
en: Once I caught him and his friends seeing who could jump the farthest off the school stage!

zh: 不过，并不是每一个学生我都能像教格雷厄姆那么成功。
en: However, I have not done as well with all my students as I have with Graham.

zh: 当老师的好处是，你可以在孩子们敞开心扉、渴望学习的时候接触到他们的思想。
en: The thing about being a teacher is that you have access to children's minds when they are open and eager to learn.

zh: 我总是不停地收到交往的网友发来的信息和最新消息。
en: I have a never-ending flow of messages and updates from all the people I associate with online.

zh: 我远非一个害羞或含蓄的人，但是我每天都在网上，我的朋友们大多也是如此。
en: I'm way beyond a shy or reserved person, but I'm wired up every day, like most of my friends.

zh: 令人真正担心的是，我认识的每个人，包括我自己，都不能够戒掉互联网。
en: What is really worrying is that no one I know, including myself, could go cold turkey.

zh: 然后，您要和其他三位评委一起讨论，并决定获得一等奖、二等奖和三等奖的选手。
en: Then you will discuss your notes with three other judges, and come to a decision about who comes first, second and third.

zh: 千里之行，始于足下。
en: A journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.

zh: 他们花一半时间想象各种发财和达到他们期望的成功的方法，另一半时间则用来考虑一旦发了财他们会干什么。
en: They spend half of their time dreaming up ways of getting rich and achieving the success they expect, and the rest of their time thinking about all the things they will do once they become rich.

zh: 为什么奥林匹克运动员要将自己逼到极限呢?
en: Why do Olympic athletes push themselves to the limits?

zh: 他担心和我一起训练不再有趣，我可能会陷入只有一枚世界冠军奖牌却无人可以分享的危险境地。
en: He was worried that I had no longer become fun to train with and that I was in danger of being left with only a world championship medal and no one to share it with.

zh: 归根结底，我天生就是个特别有干劲和意志力的人。
en: It boils down to this: I was born with an enormous amount of drive and determination.

zh: 我的生活总是走向极端。
en: I have always lived my life to extremes.

zh: 如果你愿意，可以说这不健康，但这就是我的方式，无论做什么我都会全力以赴。
en: Call it unhealthy if you want, but that is the way I roll, no matter what it is I'm applying myself to.

zh: 如果你想成为奥运冠军，就要在训练中多付出那额外的一点点，它将使你从竞争者中脱颖而出。
en: If you want to be an Olympic champion, it's all about that little extra thing you have done in your preparation that will set you apart from your competitors.

zh: 她强劲有力的打球风格为她赢得了“铁榔头”的称号。
en: Her strong and powerful playing style earned her the name “Iron Hammer”.

zh: 郎平因其成功、积极的态度和接受挑战的能力而成为中国之星。
en: Lang Ping became a star in China because of her success, her positive attitude and her ability to take on challenges.

zh: 取之有度，用之有节，则常足。
en: Take with restraint and use in moderation; this way one shall always know contentment.

zh: 物种灭绝，即某个物种的完全消失，在地球的历史上并不罕见。
en: Extinctions, where entire species are wiped out, are not unusual in our Earth's history.

zh: 虽然物种的自然灭绝并不罕见，但现在灭绝的速度让人担忧。
en: Although it is not unusual for species to die out naturally, the rate at which this is now happening is cause for concern.

zh: 我们的海洋里充斥着塑料垃圾，导致动物和鸟类因为吞食塑料而死亡。
en: Our oceans are full of plastic waste, and animals and birds end up swallowing the plastic and dying as a result.

zh: 我真的认为我们应该彻底废除商品包装。
en: I really believe we should do away with packaging completely.

zh: 有多少次我们碰到交通阻塞而动弹不得?
en: How often do we get stuck in traffic jams?

zh: 又有多少次当我们抵达工作地点或者学校时感到心力交瘁、心烦气躁呢?
en: How often do we arrive at work or school, stressed out, tired and angry?

zh: 但是和真正的社会损失相比，个人的愤怒和焦虑根本不值一提。
en: But personal anger and stress are nothing compared to the real costs socially.

zh: 别光坐在那里抱怨!
en: Don't just sit around and complain!

zh: 大家好。今天我们广播节目要谈论一些我们最特别的关系。
en: Hello, everyone. Today on our radio show we are talking about some of our most special relationships.

zh: 斯蒂芬妮，你和谁最亲近，为什么？
en: Stephanie, who are you closest to and why?

zh: 我和我妈妈最亲近，因为她完全理解并支持我。
en: I'm closest to my mother because she understands and supports me completely.

zh: 你会怎么形容她？
en: And how would you describe her?

zh: 我会说她善良、有爱心且充满热情。
en: I would say she is kind, loving and passionate.

zh: 听起来她是一位很棒的母亲。
en: She sounds like a wonderful mother.

zh: 那么，斯蒂芬妮，告诉我们一个你和你母亲最喜欢的回忆。
en: So, Stephanie, tell us about a favourite memory you have with your mother.

zh: 我有很多美好的回忆，但有一个特别的时刻是我五岁时的圣诞节。
en: I have so many great memories, but one particular occasion is Christmas time when I was five.

zh: 我非常期待圣诞节：圣诞老人带着礼物来，一顿丰盛的家庭晚餐和许多乐趣。
en: I was so looking forward to Christmas Day: Santa Claus coming with gifts, a big family dinner and lots of fun.

zh: 不幸的是，我醒来时得了严重的流感，无法下床。
en: Unfortunately, I woke up with a terrible flu and couldn't get out of bed.

zh: 你不得不错过庆祝活动。
en: You had to miss the celebration.

zh: 我错过了整整一天，感觉糟透了。
en: I missed the whole day and felt terrible.

zh: 我妈妈照顾我，然后当我在接下来的一周感觉好些时，她又举办了一次圣诞晚餐，只是为了让我能享受我错过的东西。
en: My mother took care of me and then when I was feeling better the next week, she hosted another Christmas dinner just so I could enjoy what I missed.

zh: 当我回忆起那段时光，我感到被深深地爱着。
en: When I remember that time, I feel so loved.

zh: 这太不可思议了；很明显你的母亲非常爱你！
en: That is incredible; It’s clear that your mother loves you very much!

zh: 今天我和三位不同的人交谈，了解一些别人为他们做过的最美好的事情。
en: Today I'm talking with three different people to find out some of the nicest things that people have ever done for them.

zh: 我们偶尔花点时间记住生活中的一些积极的事情是很好的，你同意吗？
en: It's good that we take some time occasionally to remember some of the positive things in life, don’t you agree?

zh: 嗨，莉莉，欢迎来到节目。
en: Hi, Lilly, welcome to the show.

zh: 那么，告诉我们你的故事。
en: So, tell us about your story.

zh: 我的故事是关于我祖母的。
en: My story is about my grandmother.

zh: 她总是对我那么好，有一件事我记得更清楚。
en: She was always so kind to me, and there's one thing I remember even more clearly.

zh: 在我毕业那天，她给了我一系列她从出生起就写给我的信。
en: On the day of my graduation, she gave me a series of letters that she had written to me since I was born.

zh: 读这些信非常感人，它们让我非常感动。
en: It was so emotional to read them and they moved me so much.

zh: 直到今天我仍然在读它们。
en: I still read them to this day.

zh: 可悲的是，她两年前去世了，但我仍然一直想着她。
en: Sadly, she passed away two years ago but I still think of her all the time.

zh: 多么美丽的故事，莉莉。
en: What a beautiful story, Lilly.

zh: 谢谢你来到这里。
en: Thank you for being here.

zh: 嗯，对我来说，是一个童年朋友。
en: Well, for me, it was a childhood friend.

zh: 我病了很长时间，所以我没能读完小学。
en: I was ill for a long time so I couldn't finish primary school.

zh: 我的朋友迈克尔在学习上帮助了我，这改变了我的生活。
en: My friend Michael helped me with my studies and it changed my life.

zh: 我能找到一份好工作，过上更好的生活。
en: I could get a good job and have a better life.

zh: 我非常感激他。
en: I'm extremely grateful to him.

zh: 你有一个多么棒的朋友啊！
en: What a wonderful friend you've had!

zh: 最后，我们有于心。
en: Finally, we have Yu Xin.

zh: 我去年遭遇了一场车祸，不得不在医院里待了几个月。
en: I was in a car accident last year and had to spend several months in the hospital.

zh: 我姐姐每天都来看我，让我重新感到快乐并对生活充满积极态度。
en: My sister visited me every day and made me feel happy again and positive about life.

zh: 因为她，我的学业从未落后，并且如医生所说，我恢复得极快。
en: Because of her, I never fell behind in schoolwork and recovered extremely fast as my doctor told me.

zh: 你们遇到的人真好啊！
en: What wonderful people you all have had!

zh: 谢谢大家的分享。
en: Thank you all for sharing.

zh: 早上好，欢迎来到“校园事务”，我是吉尔·萨姆森。
en: Good morning and welcome to "Campus Matters", I'm Jill Samson.

zh: 今天我们将探讨英语老师如何给学生反馈，以及学生对老师反馈的感受。
en: Today we're looking at how English teachers give feedback to their students and how students feel about their teachers’ feedback.

zh: 我们派马特·迪克森去校园进行调查。
en: We sent Matt Dickson out onto the campus to find out.

zh: 我们问了学生们以下问题：你的英语老师如何对你的学业提供评论或指出错误？
en: We asked the students the following questions: How does your English teacher provide comments or point out mistakes in your schoolwork?

zh: 换句话说，你通常从英语老师那里得到什么样的反馈？
en: In other words, what kind of feedback do you usually receive from your English teacher?

zh: 积极的？消极的？直接的？间接的？面对面的？
en: Positive? Negative? Direct? Indirect? Face to face?

zh: 以下是马特·迪克森发回的报道。
en: The following is the report sent back by Matt Dickson.

zh: 其中包括他在校园采访的三位学生的回应。
en: It includes the responses from three students he interviewed on campus.

zh: 嗨，我的英语老师通常在全班面前指出我们的错误或问题。
en: Hi, my English teacher usually points out our mistakes or problems in front of the whole class.

zh: 而且我不得不说，她的大部分反馈都是消极的。
en: And I have to say most of her feedback is negative.

zh: 老实说，我有时会因为她的消极反馈而感到相当受伤。
en: To be honest, I sometimes feel quite hurt by her negative feedback.

zh: 这让我对自己失去信心。
en: It makes me lose confidence in myself.

zh: 但我确实理解老师的意图。
en: But I do understand my teacher's intention.

zh: 她非常严格和要求高，因为她希望我们成为更好的学习者。
en: She is very strict and demanding because she wants us to be better learners.

zh: 我真心希望她能不那么直接，而是更鼓励人一些。
en: I really wish she could be less direct and more encouraging.

zh: 嗯，我的英语老师发现我们有任何问题，都会和我们面对面交谈。
en: Well, for any problem my English teacher finds with us, she talks to us face to face.

zh: 虽然这对我的老师来说要求很高，但它比其他类型的反馈更有效。
en: Although it is very demanding for my teacher, it is more effective than other types of feedback.

zh: 在面对面交流中，我的老师会准确地告诉我们问题所在以及如何改进。
en: During the face-to-face communication, my teacher tells us exactly where our problems are and how we can improve.

zh: 这也给了我们一个机会，对我们不理解的任何问题提出进一步的疑问。
en: It also gives us an opportunity to raise further questions about anything we don't understand.

zh: 我认为这种反馈是有帮助的。
en: I think this kind of feedback is helpful.

zh: 呃，我的英语老师经常给出非常笼统的评论，比如“好，优秀，做得好”。
en: Err, my English teacher often gives very general comments such as "good, excellent, well done".

zh: 他不会指出我的错误并提供建议。
en: He doesn't point out my mistakes and give suggestions.

zh: 然而，简单的“好”或“坏”对我来说是不够的。
en: However, a simple "good" or "bad" is not enough for me.

zh: 我想知道我的优点和缺点在哪里。
en: I want to know where my strengths and weaknesses are.

zh: 我认为老师的反馈应该是具体的。
en: I think teachers’ feedback should be specific.

zh: 例如，他可以突出我的错误，并说一些像“你需要再多练习一下过去时态”这样的话。
en: For example, he can highlight my mistakes and say things like "you need to work on the past tenses some more".

zh: 这样，我就知道我需要改进的地方了。
en: In this way, I know where I need to improve.

zh: 史密斯教授，你觉得这个广播节目怎么样？
en: Professor Smith, what do you think about the radio programme?

zh: 我觉得它相当有趣。
en: I found it rather interesting.

zh: 我理解一些学生对消极反馈的感受。
en: I understand some students’ feelings about negative feedback.

zh: 所以，我总是很小心地给予反馈。
en: So, I'm always careful about how I give feedback.

zh: 我相信最好直接指出学生的问题。
en: I believe it is best to point out students’ problems directly.

zh: 这样更有效，因为学生可以立刻抓住重点。
en: This is more effective because students can get to the point right away.

zh: 此外，我认为他们应该意识到
en: Also, I think they should realise

zh: 任何反馈都不是针对个人的。
en: that any feedback isn't personal.

zh: 嗯，提供具体的反馈是有帮助的，但非常负面的反馈可能会损害学生的信心和学习兴趣。
en: Well, it is helpful to give specific feedback, but very negative feedback might hurt students’ confidence and interest in learning.

zh: 然而，我的观点是，当学生做得不好时，他们需要习惯严厉的评论。
en: However, my opinion is that students need to get used to tough comments when they don't do good work.

zh: 我同意你的这一点，但为了更有帮助，如果我们需要给出负面反馈，我们应该以不那么直接的方式进行，尤其是对那些害羞或敏感的人。
en: I can agree with you about that, but to be more helpful, if we need to give negative feedback, we should do it in a less direct manner, especially to those who are shy or sensitive.

zh: 也许我应该试试那样做。
en: Perhaps I should try that.

zh: 我想帮助学生，但肯定不想让他们难过。
en: I want to help students but certainly don't want to upset them.

zh: 你最近看起来学习非常投入。
en: You've seemed very committed to your studies recently.

zh: 是的，我一直在做一个关于如何引领成功人生的项目。
en: Yes, I've been working on a project on how to lead a successful life.

zh: 我想每个人都想成功。
en: I suppose everyone wants to be successful.

zh: 那么你发现了什么？
en: So what have you found?

zh: 嗯，我想出了一些成功的秘诀。
en: Well, I have come up with a few tips for success.

zh: 首先，如果你想成功，你真的需要对你所做的事情充满热情和决心。
en: First, if you want to be successful, you really need to be passionate and determined about what you do.

zh: 你还需要努力工作。
en: You also need to work hard.

zh: 例如，我钦佩的一个成功人士是宇航员刘洋。
en: For example, one successful person I admire is Liu Yang, who is an astronaut.

zh: 她对太空充满热情，并且努力坚定，因此能够在很年轻的时候就成为中国第一位女宇航员。
en: She was passionate about space and determined in her effort and so was able to become China's first female astronaut at a very young age.

zh: 是的，刘洋很了不起。
en: Yes, Liu Yang is amazing.

zh: 其他成功的秘诀是相信自己并心怀感激。
en: Other secrets of success are to believe in yourself and to be grateful.

zh: 我理解相信自己，但心怀感激有什么帮助呢？
en: I understand believing in yourself, but how does being grateful help?

zh: 嗯，心怀感激意味着你总是感激你所拥有的以及别人为你所做的一切。
en: Well, being grateful means you're always appreciative of what you have and what other people have done for you.

zh: 这样，你很可能对生活保持积极态度，并能激励他人。
en: In this way, you are likely to stay positive about life and be inspiring to others.

zh: 是的，这很有道理。
en: Yes, that makes a lot of sense.

zh: 最后一个建议是从失败中学习。
en: The final tip is to learn from failure.

zh: 每个人都可能失败，但重要的是你如何应对失败。
en: Everyone can fail but it's how you deal with failure that counts.

zh: 这让我想起了田径运动员刘翔。
en: That reminds me of Liu Xiang, the track athlete.

zh: 尽管职业生涯中多次受伤，但他始终保持决心和专注。
en: Despite many injuries in his career, he always remained determined and focused.

zh: 一个成功的人总是保持投入和专注。
en: A successful person always remains committed and focused.

zh: 现在，让我们自己尝试并遵循这些建议吧！
en: Now, let's try and follow these tips ourselves!

zh: 谈到成功，娜塔莉，你认为个人发展中最重要的方面是什么？
en: Talking about being successful, Natalie, what would you say is the most important aspect of personal development?

zh: 我认为成为一个更好的人很重要。
en: I believe becoming a better person is important.

zh: 我认为接下来要做的就是不断学习。
en: I think the next thing is to always keep learning.

zh: 一个成功的人永远不会停止学习。
en: A successful person never stops learning.

zh: 在那之后，我想说的是，与他人良好合作很重要。
en: After that, I would say it's important to work well with others.

zh: 没有它，什么也做不成。
en: Without it, nothing really gets done.

zh: 通过与他人合作，你可以培养许多关键技能，如合作、沟通、达成共识等。
en: By working with others you develop a lot of key skills, cooperation, communication, building agreement and others.

zh: 那么，关于在办公室花费大量时间的旧观念……现在这不重要了吗？
en: So, what about the old idea of spending many hours in the office ... Is that unimportant now?

zh: 嗯，努力工作仍然很重要，但现在你可以在任何地方工作。
en: Well, it is still important to work hard but now you can work everywhere and anywhere.

zh: 在一些公司，员工可以在家工作，或者在公园，或者任何他们喜欢的地方工作。
en: In some companies, employees can work from home, or the park or anywhere they like.

zh: 努力工作和在办公室不是一回事。
en: Working hard and being in the office are not the same thing.

zh: 你想成功吗？
en: Do you want to be successful?

zh: 我相信大多数年轻人都有一个梦想。
en: I believe most young people have a dream.

zh: 遵循我们的五个秘诀，你就能做到！
en: Follow our five secrets and you can do it!

zh: 首先，对你所做的事情充满热情。
en: First, be passionate about what you do.

zh: 如果你做某事主要是出于热爱而不是其他原因，那么它很可能会引领你走向成功。
en: If you do something primarily for love and not for other reasons, probably it will lead you to success.

zh: 以杂交水稻之父、农业领域最具影响力的科学家之一袁隆平为例。
en: Take Yuan Longping, the father of hybrid rice, one of the most influential scientists in agriculture, for example.

zh: 50多年来，他毕生致力于水稻生产，他的研究不仅帮助中国找到了消除饥饿的方法，也为世界和平与社会进步做出了贡献。
en: For more than 50 years, he has devoted his life to rice production and his research has not only helped China find a way to remove hunger but also contributed to world peace and social progress.

zh: 当马云第一次有辞去大学教师工作、创办一家在线购物公司的想法时，大家都认为他疯了。
en: When Ma Yun first had the idea of quitting his job as a college teacher and starting up an online shopping company, everybody thought he was crazy.

zh: 但他相信这家公司会成功。
en: But he was confident that this company would be successful.

zh: 如今，阿里巴巴不仅成为全球最大的在线销售公司，也改变了人们的购物习惯。
en: Now Alibaba has not only become the biggest online sales company in the world but also changes people's shopping habits as well.

zh: 我的第三个建议是：努力工作。
en: My third tip is: work hard.

zh: “成功的万小时法则”指出，你需要练习至少一万个小时。
en: The "10,000-hour rule for success" states that you need to practise for a minimum of 10,000 hours.

zh: 这大约是五年里每天六个小时。
en: That's about six hours a day for five years.

zh: 比尔·盖茨就是一个很好的例子。
en: Bill Gates is a good example.

zh: 大约五年时间，在他十几岁的时候，他花了一万个小时在一台基础计算机上工作。
en: For about five years, when he was a teenager, he spent 10,000 hours working on a basic computer.

zh: 他的努力使他比别人有很大的优势，他在20岁时就成为微软的创始人之一。
en: His hard work gave him a big advantage over other people and he became one of the founders of Microsoft by the age of 20.

zh: 我的第四个建议是：设定高目标。
en: My tip number four is: set your goals high.

zh: 五岁时，诺瓦克·德约科维奇就决定他不想只做一个优秀的网球运动员，他想成为世界上最好的网球运动员。
en: At the age of five, Novak Djokovic decided he didn't want to be just a good tennis player, he wanted to be the best tennis player in the world.

zh: 2011年，24岁的德约科维奇赢得了三项主要的世界冠军，并成为世界第一。
en: In 2011, Djokovic, aged 24, won three major world championships and became the World Number One.

zh: 最后，我最后一个建议是：不断尝试。
en: Finally, my last tip is: keep trying.

zh: 对哈利·波特的粉丝来说幸运的是，作者J.K.罗琳没有理会那十二家拒绝她书籍的出版商的负面评论。
en: Luckily for Harry Potter fans, the author J. K. Rowling didn't listen to the negative comments from the twelve publishers who rejected her books.

zh: 两年来，她一直试图让它们出版。
en: For two years, she tried to get them published.

zh: 甚至与她签约的出版商也说她在儿童书籍方面没有赚钱的机会。
en: Even the publisher who gave her a contract said she had no chance of making money in children's books.

zh: 他错了，在过去的十年里，J·K·罗琳一直是世界上最著名的儿童读物作者。
en: He was wrong and for the last ten years, J. K. Rowling has been the author of the most famous children's books in the world.

zh: 这些成功人士从底层开始，登上了顶峰。
en: These successful people started at the bottom and got to the top.

zh: 遵循这五个秘诀，你就会成功！
en: Follow the five secrets and you will succeed!

zh: 我一直在读一本关于如何成功的书，它提供了很多很棒的建议。
en: I've been reading a book that gives lots of great tips on how to be successful.

zh: 我认为它非常有用且激励人心。
en: I think it’s very useful and motivating.

zh: 哦？这本书叫什么名字？
en: Oh? What's the name of this book?

zh: 这本书叫《成功的秘诀》。
en: The book is called Secrets of Success.

zh: 请告诉我关于它的事。
en: Please tell me about it.

zh: 你学到了哪些东西？
en: What kinds of things have you learnt?

zh: 嗯，首先，这本书鼓励人们为成功做好准备。
en: Well, first, the book encourages people to prepare themselves for success.

zh: 例如，通过想象他们最好的自己，并写下他们的目标。
en: For example, by imaging their best possible self and by writing down their goals.

zh: 真的吗？我对此很感兴趣。
en: Really? I'm interested in it.

zh: 我可能得看一看。
en: I may have to give it a look.

zh: 你还从书中学到了什么？
en: What else did you learn from the book?

zh: 所以第二，我学到了如何通过积极的态度培养好习惯的技巧，例如，专注于你所做的事情。
en: So second, I learnt tips on how to develop good habits through a positive attitude, for example, being focused on what you do.

zh: 哇，听起来像是一本有用的书。
en: Wow, it sounds like a useful book.

zh: 我现在真的很想看看它。
en: I really want to check it out now.

zh: 好的，第三，它提到了有效的日常实践，比如制定待办事项清单，限制在社交网络和其他干扰上的时间。
en: OK and thirdly, it mentions effective daily practices such as making to-do lists and limiting time on social networking and other distractions.

zh: 嗯，你说服我去读这本书了！
en: Mm, you've convinced me to read this book!

zh: 你看完后我能借你的那本吗？
en: Can I borrow your copy when you have finished reading it?

zh: 当然，我很乐意借给你。
en: Sure, I'd be glad to lend it to you.

zh: 你昨天去听的那个讲座怎么样？
en: So how was that lecture you went to yesterday?

zh: 是环境专家琳达·魏教授主讲的，对吧？
en: It was by Professor Linda Wei, the environmental expert, wasn't it?

zh: 是的，真的信息量很大！
en: Yes, it was really informative!

zh: 我学到了很多关于世界现在面临的环境挑战。
en: I learnt a lot about the environmental challenges the world is facing now.

zh: 多告诉我一些。
en: Tell me more about it.

zh: 嗯，如你所知，我强烈地感觉到我们需要改变我们的生活方式来保护我们的星球。
en: Well, as you know, I really feel strongly that we need to change the way we live in order to protect our planet.

zh: 讲座对此谈了很多。
en: The lecture talked a lot about this.

zh: 嗯，首先，魏教授对使用清洁能源非常热情。
en: Well, first, Professor Wei was very passionate about using clean energy.

zh: 听完讲座后，我明白了石油和煤炭的使用是对我们星球最大的威胁之一。
en: After the lecture, I can see that the use of oil and coal is one of the biggest threats to our planet.

zh: 我们绝对需要尽快转向不同形式的能源。
en: We definitely need to switch to different forms of power as soon as possible.

zh: 确实如此。她有没有提到关于使用塑料袋的事情？
en: That's very true. Did she say anything about using plastic bags?

zh: 是的。她谈到了塑料垃圾如何正在毁灭我们的海洋。
en: Yes. She talked about how plastic waste is killing our oceans.

zh: 这很清楚——我们都需要回收利用并减少我们产生的废物量。
en: It's very clear — we all need to recycle and reduce the amount of waste we produce.

zh: 我完全同意。我认为我们应该减少不可回收塑料的使用来保护我们的星球。
en: I totally agree. I think we should cut down the use of unrecyclable plastic to protect our planet.

zh: 这是个好主意。但当然，正如教授所说：“我们都在保护环境方面扮演着角色。如果我们都尽自己的一份力，我们就能在地球上过上更健康的生活。”
en: That's a good idea. But of course, as the professor said: "We all have a role to play in protecting the environment. If we all do our part, we can live a healthier life on Earth."

zh: 今天能有布拉德肖博士和我们在一起，我确实感到非常幸运。
en: I feel very lucky indeed to have Dr. Bradshaw with us today.

zh: 他从事保护濒危动物的工作。
en: He works on protecting animals in danger.

zh: 目前，他正在参与一个犀牛保护项目。
en: At present, he is in a rhino protecting programme.

zh: 是的，许多动物因其身上的某些东西而被非法猎杀。
en: Yes, many animals are illegally hunted for something they have.

zh: 就犀牛而言，是它们的角。
en: In the rhino's case, it is their horns.

zh: 有些人认为它在医学上有用。
en: Some people think it is useful in medicine.

zh: 嗯，第一步是教育——我们需要让每个人都知道猎杀这些动物是非法的，并且这些神奇的动物是我们所生活的世界的一部分。
en: Well, the first step is education — we need to make everyone know hunting these animals is illegal and that these amazing animals are part of the world we live in.

zh: 如果我们现在不采取行动，20年后世界上将不再有犀牛。
en: If we don't do something now, there will be no rhinos left in the world in 20 years.

zh: 这是一个极其紧迫的问题……
en: That's an incredibly urgent matter ...

zh: 在政府公布其新环境计划的那一周，凯文·麦凯布和艾丽西亚·斯图尔特思考了食品包装浪费的问题。
en: In the week when the government sets out its new environmental programme, Kevin McCabe and Alicia Stewart consider the problem of food packaging waste.

zh: 昨天我下班回家的路上，我在当地一家超市停了下来。
en: On my way home from work yesterday, I stopped off at a local supermarket.

zh: 除了其他东西，我还想买几个椰子。
en: Among other things, I wanted to buy a couple of coconuts.

zh: 当我拿起它们时，我很惊讶每个椰子都用透明薄塑料包裹着。
en: When I picked them up, I was amazed that each coconut was wrapped in clear, thin plastic.

zh: 这难道不是有史以来最愚蠢的包装例子吗？
en: Isn't this the most stupid example of packaging ever?

zh: 你选了一个不寻常的包装例子，但这有其原因。
en: You've picked an unusual example of packaging, but there is a reason for this.

zh: 超市希望确保椰子以最佳状态到达消费者手中。
en: Supermarkets want to make sure that coconuts reach the consumer in the best condition.

zh: 包装有助于保持产品新鲜；这意味着产品的保质期会更长。
en: The packaging helps to keep the product fresh; it means the product will last longer.

zh: 我理解这一点，但在这种情况下，这完全是浪费和不必要的。
en: I understand that, but in this case it's completely wasteful and unnecessary.

zh: 我的意思是，为什么要把椰子用塑料包起来？
en: I mean, why wrap a coconut in plastic?

zh: 它已经有自己的天然包装了。
en: It already has its own natural packaging.

zh: 它有一个几乎不可能打破的坚硬外壳！
en: It's got a tough shell that's almost impossible to break!

zh: 是的，但塑料包装还有其他用途。
en: Yes, but the plastic wrapping also has other uses.

zh: 例如，它可以防止椰子的毛在运输过程中进入其他食物。
en: For example, it stops coconut hairs from getting into other food during transport.

zh: 并且它在运输过程中保持椰子的清洁。
en: And it keeps the coconut clean during the transportation process.

zh: 真的有人会担心椰子上的那一点泥土吗？
en: Is anyone really worried about a bit of dirt on a coconut?

zh: 那环境呢？
en: And what about the environment?

zh: 很多用于包装的塑料从未被回收。
en: A lot of the plastic used in packaging never recycles.

zh: 我们的海洋充满了塑料垃圾，动物和鸟类最终会吞食塑料并因此死亡。
en: Our oceans are full of plastic waste, and animals and birds end up swallowing the plastic and dying as a result.

zh: 我不得不说我同意你的观点。
en: I have to say I agree with you on that.

zh: 有些食品包装无法回收，这不够好——包装应该被改变，使其可回收。
en: Some food packaging cannot be recycled, and that's not good enough — the packaging should be changed so that it's recyclable.

zh: 但让我们现实一点：我们仍然需要包装。
en: But let's be realistic: We still need packaging.

zh: 我们只需要使用正确的包装类型。
en: We just need to use the right kind of packaging.

zh: 我认为我们需要一个更具创新性的解决方案。
en: I think we need a more innovative solution.

zh: 我真的认为我们应该完全取消包装。
en: I really believe we should do away with packaging completely.

zh: 德国有一家超市完全没有产品包装。
en: There's a supermarket in Germany that has no product packaging at all.

zh: 它的顾客购物时自带可重复填充的容器，并用它们代替塑料盒和袋子。
en: Its customers take refillable containers with them when they shop, and use them instead of plastic boxes and bags.

zh: 这听起来是个好主意，但我想知道有多少人真的在那里购物。
en: It sounds like a wonderful idea, but I wonder how many people really shop there.

zh: 我不确定大多数消费者会想以那种方式购物。
en: I'm not sure most consumers would want to shop that way.

zh: 明天是垃圾收集日。
en: It's rubbish collection day tomorrow.

zh: 我会把我们的垃圾桶拿出去。
en: I'll put out our bins.

zh: 哇，我们有这么多垃圾！
en: Wow, we have so much rubbish!

zh: 一想到我们对环境造成的影响，我就感到焦虑。
en: I just get anxious when I think of the impact we have on the environment.

zh: 我们怎么在一周内就产生了这么多？
en: How can we have produced so much in just one week?

zh: 看看所有这些塑料袋！
en: Look at all these plastic bags!

zh: 我们去超市时真的应该购买可重复使用的购物袋。
en: We really should buy reusable shopping bags when we go to the supermarket.

zh: 那将是我们减少浪费的一个良好开端。
en: That would be a good start to reduce our waste.

zh: 我也注意到我们有多少外卖食品包装。
en: I also notice how much takeaway food packaging we have.

zh: 我们应该尝试更经常自己做饭。
en: We should try to cook for ourselves more often.

zh: 看看所有这些纸盒和塑料餐具！
en: Look at all these cartons and plastic cutlery!

zh: 在家做饭对环境友好得多。
en: Cooking at home is much more environmentally friendly.

zh: 嗯，我做饭很糟糕，所以对我来说在家做饭可能很难，但我想从现在开始，我会告诉餐厅在我点餐时不要给我任何塑料餐具、筷子和纸盘。
en: Well, I'm a terrible cook so it might be hard for me to cook at home, but I think I will tell restaurants not to give me any plastic cutlery, chopsticks and paper plates when I order from now on.

zh: 这听起来是个好的开始。
en: That sounds like a good start.

zh: 你知道我们在垃圾分类方面也很差——比如看看这张报纸。
en: You know we are also quite bad at sorting our rubbish — look at this newspaper for example.

zh: 它本可以很容易地被回收，但我们却把它扔掉了。
en: It could easily be recycled but we threw it away.

zh: 我们还扔掉了许多玻璃瓶。
en: And we threw away lots of glass bottles too.

zh: 从现在开始，让我们把垃圾分类并送到回收中心。
en: Let's sort our rubbish and take it to a recycling centre from now on.

zh: 当地市政委员会将帮助 reuse 和 recycle 它。
en: The local council will help to reuse and recycle it.

zh: 我认为这些都是我们可以采取的、现实且可实现的步骤，以帮助保护环境。
en: I think these are all realistic and achievable steps that we can take to help protect the environment.

zh: 我也刚在网上看了，有很多我们可以加入的团体，它们分享关于如何减少和回收的想法。
en: I've just looked online too and there are lots of groups we could join which share ideas on how to reduce and recycle.

zh: 知道有这么多其他人都致力于为我们星球的更美好未来做出贡献，感觉真好。
en: It feels good knowing that there are so many other people who are committed to contributing to a better future for our planet.
